KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_RegretPeacekeeperDiary_Page_:
November 28, 2025

I can't take it anymore.

We were ordered to... Fuck! Fuck! Fuck! FUCK!!!

They made us turn some people away from the evacuation. Not just individuals, but whole families... whole villages. This is a nightmare.

And when I complained to the higher-ups, they threatened me and said they would fire me for disobedience.

Fuck!

Today, I had to deny a father and his little daughter. She was as old as my child.

I’m not stupid. I know what's going on.

Fucking hell, I have to do something, or I'll regret it for the rest of my life... not again. Not another '94.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_SawmillWorkersDiary_Page_1:
November 25, 2025

I can’t stop thinking about what I saw yesterday. It was supposed to be just another night shift at the sawmill, but everything changed when that thing appeared in the sky.

It was so otherworldly strange, I don't even know how to describe it. The whole crew stopped working. Some of the men stood there, staring. Others started shouting, yelling things I couldn’t make out. The air felt heavy, like the forest itself was holding its breath.

That’s when the real panic started. One of the new guys dropped his chainsaw and ran. Another started screaming. No one wanted to stay.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_SawmillWorkersDiary_Page_2:
By morning, the sawmill was almost empty. The boss was furious, barking at anyone who’d listen, but even he looked shaken. I can’t blame anyone for leaving. Whatever that thing was, it didn’t feel safe to stay.

I’ve worked here my whole life, but now… I don’t think I can do it anymore. The forest doesn’t feel the same, and I don’t think I’ll ever look at the sky without feeling that strange weight in my chest.

Tomorrow, I’m going to pack my things. Let someone else take my place. I’m done.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_PuzzledGirlsDiary_Page_1:
November 25, 2025

Dear Diary,

Today was so strange. Mama said we were going on a big trip, so we came to the airfield. It was really loud, and there were lots of people everywhere, way more than the last time we flew. But no one looked happy.

I saw a man running, holding so many bags that he dropped one, and stuff went everywhere. He looked so scared. A lady tried to help him pick it up, but he yelled at her and kept running. Why was he so mad? Mama pulled me closer and told me not to stare, but I couldn’t stop looking.

There was a baby crying so loud. It made my ears hurt. The mama was holding the baby so tight, and she kept looking around like she was waiting for something bad to happen. I asked Mama if the baby was okay, but she just said Shh.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_PuzzledGirlsDiary_Page_2:
Some people were yelling at a man in a uniform. He didn’t yell back, just kept waving his hands and pointing. His face was so red. One man tried to push past him, but two other men came and made him stop.

Mama was squeezing my hand really hard, so I asked if she was mad. She shook her head but didn’t say anything.

I wanted to ask Mama why everyone looked so scared, but she told me to be quiet. Then she picked me up and hugged me tight. Her heart was beating really fast, and her arms were shaking a little.

I don’t think I like it here anymore.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_LostTouristsDiary_Page_1:
OMG, diary! This trip has been like, the most amazing thing ever! Southeast Asia is so wild and different—it’s nothing like back home, but I am LIVING for it. The food is spicy, the views are insane, and don’t even get me started on how cute the little scooters are. (Yes, I almost crashed mine, but let’s not talk about that).

This week has been jam-packed! Yesterday, I hiked up this gorgeous hill that overlooks a tiny village—so sweaty, but the view was totally worth it for the pics. This morning, I wandered through a local market, trying all kinds of weird and wonderful snacks. You know me, always up for an adventure! And then I made it finally to the Blue Lagoon. Like, HELLOOO postcard vibes! The water is refreshing and just sooo blue. I couldn't stop staring.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_LostTouristsDiary_Page_2:
So, get this. I decided to camp out here—yes, me, camping. I know, I’m as shocked as you are! But it’s kinda cozy under the stars, with the sound of the water nearby. I met this super chill group of locals who totally know how to have a good time. Like, I never knew that even in a small country like Lamang, you can meet cool people like them.

After a little bonfire (and some questionable singing), they said they’d show me their “special place”. I have no idea what that means, but they promised it’s something unforgettable. I’m all in! :)

Life is just... so much bigger than I thought. I can’t wait to see what’s next!

KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_TouristsDiaryBadExperience_P:
Blue Lagoon Experience

Today was supposed to be a highlight of my Southeast Asian tour—a serene dip in the famed Blue Lagoon, nestled in the heart of lush jungles. Instead, it has been a day of awkward glares, terse exchanges, and mounting discomfort.

The place itself is beautiful, as picturesque as the brochures promised. The kind of place that makes you forget the world for a while. But any charm it holds evaporates the moment you interact with the locals.

From the moment I arrived, I felt like an unwelcome guest. My driver grumbled as he dropped me off, muttering something in the local language that didn’t sound remotely friendly. At the entrance, a group of men loitered, their expressions unreadable but their presence distinctly unnerving.

When I tried to ask about the best spots for photos, they exchanged looks and chuckled—not the warm, inclusive laughter of people enjoying life, but something... off. Maybe I’m overthinking it, but their mocking tones felt deliberate.

Lunch wasn’t much better. I ordered a dish from a roadside stall, only for the vendor to serve me with a scowl that could curdle the broth. My attempts to engage her in polite conversation were met with silence, her eyes darting warily toward the group of men lounging nearby.

Even in the water, I couldn’t shake the unease. Everything felt oddly quiet, the kind of quiet that prickles your skin. It wasn’t crowded, yet the few locals present seemed more interested in watching me than enjoying the swimming themselves.

I had heard whispers from other travelers that Lamang’s hospitality can be... selective. Something about a deep-rooted distrust of outsiders. Perhaps there’s truth to it. I tried to reassure myself it’s just their culture, but the lingering tension felt too personal, too directed.

I decided to abandon my plans to explore more of the famous Blue Lagoon. The beauty of this place can’t compete with the weight of the odd stares. Lamang’s landscapes may be enchanting, but its people—or at least those here—seem particularly dubious.

Perhaps tomorrow will be better. But tonight I'll sleep with one eye open.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_ScaredPiratesDiary_Page_1:
December 21, 2025

This fucking place feels wrong.

The wind howls through the empty streets, and the creaking of the old fishing boats left behind is enough to make anyone jump. It seemed like the perfect hideout after we were pushed out of Tiger Bay, but now I'm beginning to wonder if we made a mistake.

At night, the sounds are the worst, like whispers or something... I've been keeping to myself, staying inside when the fog rolls in. The villagers seem to have mysteriously disappeared. They left everything behind, but no one is taking anything. Ironic, considering we are pirates. There's something wrong with this place… something unnatural.

I've seen enough to know when to leave, but right now, we have nowhere else to go...

KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_LocalsDiaryFrogs_Page_1:
January 2, 2026

I can hear them everywhere. Frogs, frogs, frogs. Nonstop serenade of frog croaking. At first, I hated it, but recently things changed.

I started to hear a pattern, a trace of a... It's hard to describe; maybe an echo of a message? The more I listen to the frogs, the more I understand that there is something to be understood. They are telling a secret that all can hear, but no one will be able to comprehend. They laugh at our incompetence.

I need to know more. I must have more frogs. More exposure to their croaking. The truth is out there and I am so close to discovering it.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_NamThavenDoctorsDiary_Page_1:
December 28, 2025

I try to do what I can, but the lack of supplies gnaws at me. There’s no clean water, barely any antibiotics, and I ran out of bandages last week. I’ve been tearing up sheets and boiling them for reuse. It’s humiliating. A doctor should heal, not improvise with scraps like a desperate tailor.

The people here aren’t just fighting wounds or infections; they’re battling despair. I see it in their eyes. The question they’re too afraid to ask: Is survival even worth it?

I keep telling myself that if I can save just one more life, this nightmare will have meaning. But tonight I can’t help but wonder if even I believe that anymore.

January 14, 2026

The thugs who have taken over this town are nothing short of ruthless, yet today they brought me a man—one of their own—barely breathing, his body riddled with stab wounds from some internal feud.

They barged into the clinic, weapons drawn, barking demands. Saying no wasn’t an option.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_NamThavenDoctorsDiary_Page_2:
I saved that man… and it disgusts me. But what choice did I have? My hands save lives. That’s my purpose. I keep asking myself, where do you draw the line? Do I keep patching up monsters, knowing they’ll use the strength I give them to terrorize the innocent?

…I hate my life.

February 10, 2026

It is nothing short of a miracle, but a young woman from a foreign PMC contacted me and offered some much-needed help.

Without her, I don't know how I could continue treating the remaining locals. She brought everything I needed. Bandages, antibiotics, clean syringes. Things I had almost forgotten the feel of. She even brought painkillers, real ones, not the makeshift concoctions I've been forced to brew.

I don't know where she got the supplies, but I have learned not to ask too many questions.

The town mayn't be doomed after all.

KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_MysteriousCodebook_Page_1:
November 1, 2025

I have arrived on the island and settled in. The station is operational, as ordered. Now, all that remains is to wait for further instructions.

November 8, 2025

The clock struck thirteen when the sparrows flew east—three of them, not four. The red suitcase remained unopened, but the key had gone missing again.

The rivers now flow uphill. It began after the third chime, but no one, except the cat, noticed, continuing to watch the crane. Instructions arrived, folded into a paper crane; the ink had smudged, but the message was clear: “Follow the shadow at dawn.”

November 15, 2025

I marked the tree with five scratches, though I doubt it will matter when the tide comes in. Today, the jungle whispers in silence—something about the glass cracking.

It is already midnight. The stars are winking too often to be trusted. I think they know about the soup.

November 23, 2025

Finally, an end to the boredom. New orders have arrived, and it seems my combat experience from Africa will finally come in handy. I’ve gathered everything I need and am leaving the base as instructed. The jungle awaits me ahead.

New recruits have arrived. Mostly escaped criminals and desperate civilians. Should make it a lot easier to keep an eye on the LAF cunts. No big movements from them yet, but let's not get too comfy.

January 6, 2026

Picked up some juicy comms chatter out of Fort Narith. Sounds like they're beefing up patrols on the west side of the jungle. Something spooked them. Anyway, if you're running supplies, stick to the back roads.

February 12, 2026

Big news! We're gearing up for the east side op. Everyone needs to be ready—gear, weapons, EVERYTHING. It's gonna be huge. They got numbers, sure, but we got the element of surprise. We'll hit them where it hurts. Make sure your comms work and pack extra ammo. No excuses!

KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_RebelNotebook_Page_1:
February 15, 2026

The time has come to strike at the heart of their cruelty. The Hole. This fucking place has long been one of their biggest weapons against our people. We will shatter that weapon once and for all and free those trapped within its depths.

Our preparations are nearly complete. The scouts returned with vital intelligence today. Nothing stands in the way of our victory. Every rebel knows his role. Supplies are stretched thin, but we’ve managed to arm everyone with what little we have left.

I’ve ordered one last briefing before nightfall. Every step, every contingency must be clear. I cannot risk even the slightest misunderstanding; too much is at stake. If we succeed, we’ll not only free the captives, but strike a blow so loud that even the government will feel its tremors.

Failure is not an option. This is more than a battle, it is a declaration of our will. For every life lost inside that bunker, for every scream it has swallowed, we will bring justice. Tomorrow, Yaderny Bunker Lamang 1 will no longer stand as a symbol of fear. It will be a symbol of our resistance.

Free Lamang!

I think I’ll go insane! Due to some crazy coincidence, I became a gang leader… ME of all people! What have I done to deserve something like this? Thankfully, I've seen some gangster movies, so I can mimic the behavior of these types of people. The boys will manage without me, and I can finally live the life I’ve always wanted.

KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_EncryptedLogbook_Page_1:
The sky at dawn turned clear, like an overripe mandarin. I stepped into my boots and found them full of clock hands and the scent of yesterday’s arguments. The trees outside the window were arguing in Morse code—mostly insults, some warnings. I took notes, but the paper grew teeth and laughed at me.
*
Breakfast was a soap bubble served with a fork. But I ate it anyway. My reflection whispered something I couldn’t catch, so I kissed the mirror and didn’t apologize to anyone. The wind carried a chair across the yard. It asked if I wanted a ride. I declined. I wasn’t dressed for existential transport.
*
I found a map inside a hollow potato. It led to a place that does not exist on calm days. I took a shovel and a melody clenched between my teeth. I followed both. Everything smelled of copper and disbelief.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_EncryptedLogbook_Page_2:
The moon refused to rise tonight, saying it was tired of watching. The stars mumbled something incoherent. I packed the important things — three socks, a photograph of a lizard I’ve never met, and a box that hums when no one is looking. I buried them near the crooked tree with iron roots. There is no time. I think they will come back for the gift.
*
The ink slips, as if afraid. I don’t know what we buried, only that it wasn’t meant to stay above ground for long. Something hums beneath the soil. Maybe.

Two places surface in the fragments:
North — LAF, pulsing with interference and old warnings.
East — where metal dreams rot.

I can’t say for certain which one it’s in. Only that we hid it quickly. Too quickly. The cargo. Handle it with care.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_FahsDiary_Page_1:
The boss was giving another lecture on good manners and better customer service. Yeah, daddy, I know what to do and what not to do. And when I do what I'm not supposed to do, I do it secretly. It's not the first time I've stolen.
*
What a boring shift! Two lousy locals and a fat Thai tourist. They had nothing worth shit! What the fuck am I supposed to do with a pen and a bone pendant?
*
The guy was weird. He gave me the creeps, but on the other hand, he was so polite. He even offered me some coke. Of course, I took him up on it. Who can say no to free drugs, right? I almost felt guilty when I searched his wallet while he slept. When I saw the amount of money he had, I took just a little bit. And all the time, I felt like he was watching me, wondering if he should snap my neck or leave me alive. I still get shivers down my spine.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_FahsDiary_Page_2:
Fucking Yanks don't know how to party. They just get piss drunk and throw up on the floor. Europeans, on the other hand, know their limits. I still have fond memories of the two brothers from Slovakia. They were so much fun! Anyway, I managed to snatch two phones, a laptop, and a key chain.
*
Okay, I know I shouldn't, but I just couldn't resist taking the black beret. I'm sure the guy won't need it. The LAF will give him a new one. Besides, he deserves it! They do terrible shit to us, ordinary people. They fucking do whatever the fuck they want! Consider it a small act of rebellion against the regime.
*
Looks like I won't have to buy coke for a while. One of the suckers had some white powder on him, so I appropriated it like the whites appropriated our island in the past. I bet it's some high class shit! In any case, it will serve its purpose.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_TorturersNotebook_Page_1:
Commenced our little conversation. The guests are not cooperating as befits their situation. You'd think seeing what happened to their friends would make them wiser.
*
The filthy sellouts are denying all allegations. It seems it will take more of my craft to make them talk.
*
We tried everything, but all the evidence suggests that these idiots didn’t collaborate with the foreign mercenaries. Nevertheless, their deaths were a necessary sacrifice for the reconstruction of our homeland and for our cause.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_MedicsDiary_Page_1:
The easy takeover of Fort Narith turned into an unforeseen massacre. Almost all of the guys suffered some kind of injury — gunshot wounds, shrapnel trauma, pneumothorax, blast concussions, deep lacerations from glass and metal, compound fractures. What the fuck went wrong? Kaptei was adamant he had solid intel that the place would be practically empty and ripe for the taking. All that hardware just sitting there, completely unguarded. One last job to set us up for life. We dragged our asses here all the way from Cambodia, and this is how it fucking ends?
*
I have to work in these shitty conditions, with people dying every day due to a lack of medical supplies, and now, to make matters worse, some unknown fucking disease is rapidly spreading through our ranks. The men are coming in dehydrated, feverish, and vomiting nonstop, with severe diarrhea and abdominal cramps. This job has turned into a nightmare.
*
The situation is critical, and Kaptei doesn’t want to hear it. He is obsessed and will send us all to our deaths. I’m not the only one who sees this, and I cannot wait anymore. Today is the day. The river will be our salvation.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_DiaryOfHabits_Page_1:
I had to start this diary because belief in Phi Pa Mum is growing on its own, completely separate from my teachings. It's fascinating to see how a faith can take on a life of its own.
*
Some of the villagers have started spreading rumors that Phi Pa Mum is actually an alien being from the stars. A pretty wild — but interesting — interpretation of this so-called almighty deity.
*
I found a warning note posted in the village, saying Phi Pa Mum is watching every step we take. Honestly, it’s good to see people showing some fear and respect.
*
Today, Mali told me about a dream she had. She said Phi Pa Mum revealed that all true believers will be saved from a coming catastrophe headed for the island. I just hope she doesn’t start a panic — that’s the last thing we need messing with our business.

Entry Number .................46553512

Meeting held on ................. November 23, 2025

Operat. Officer .................25085

1. Kob stated that his neighbors (Mr. and Mrs. Nang) made anti-government remarks while playing petanque together. Specifically, Mr. Nang is said to have made the following comments (quote): "We invite foreigners who treat us worse than animals, and no one does anything about it. No wonder the young ones join the LLA."

Based on what we know, this information about Mr. and Ms. Nang's statements seems plausible, as we have reports from other informants who have testified similarly.

The standard strategy of progressive re-education will not produce the desired results in this case. Mr. and Mrs. Nang refer to the LLA terrorist group in their anti-state speeches, thus expressing a degree of sympathy for these criminals. Given the recurrence of these anti-state speeches and the obvious sympathy for the LLA, it is advisable to skip the first stage of re-education and go straight to strong action and isolate Mr. and Mrs. Nang from the rest of society.

2. Kob is a reliable source, and this is not the first time he has helped convict dangerous anti-state elements. Given his past and present track record, we propose that application number 2111678952 be granted and that his daughter be allowed to study at a foreign university.

CAPTURE OF UNIDENTIFIED FOREIGN COMBATANT

Summary:

Our soldiers stationed at the western GZ entry point apprehended an unidentified male. The individual was taken into custody without incident.

Details:

Upon capture, the subject was non-compliant with standard identification procedures. He refused to provide his name, serial number, or unit affiliation. Interrogation protocols were initiated as per standing orders regarding foreign combatants.

Despite multiple sessions, the subject remained uncooperative. When questioned about his ties to the three known foreign private military companies currently active in our country, the detainee responded with laughter. No identifying insignia or documentation was found on his person.

Escape:

The subject successfully escaped confinement under unknown circumstances. An order for recapture was not given.

KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_FL_PLOfficialLAFDocument_Page_1:
Fort Narith - Increased Terrorist Activity

Summary:

Terrorist forces operating in the jungle area around Fort Narith have significantly escalated their operations. Recently, our units have recorded four direct engagements. It is imminent that LLA guerrilla forces are strengthening their positions and establishing new bases.

Key Findings:

A captured terrorist confirmed plans for further attacks on Fort Narith.

The remaining civilians are helping the LLA terrorists, so increased pressure is desirable.

Recommendation:

Continued monitoring is critical to disrupt further encampment construction. Given the scale and coordination of terrorist activity, it is imperative that operational focus be prioritized toward containing and neutralizing this immediate threat. While doing so, monitoring of other organizations (e.g., mercenaries and criminal gangs) should continue at a reduced but sufficient level to prevent exploitation of our redirected attention. Strategic emphasis must remain on the LLA terrorists, without fully neglecting parallel threats.

Interrogation Transcript

Interrogator:
Mr. Hoang, we have evidence that you sabotaged military vehicles at Fort Narith. What can you tell me about that?

Sith H.:
Nothing. It's a fabrication. I'm just an ordinary mechanic doing my job.

Interrogator:
Mr. Hoang, we have eyewitnesses.

Sith H.:
In that case it's total bullshit.

Interrogator:
Mr. Hoang, are you an LLA sympathizer?

Sith H.:
No.

Interrogator:
We have evidence that you have expressed anti-government sentiments in the past. If you don't like the government, why did you apply for a job as a mechanic at Fort Narith?

Sith H.:
I didn't apply. You guys approached me.

Interrogator:
Mr. Hoang, are you paid by foreign agents?

Sith H.:
No.

Interrogator:
Mr. Hoang, please cooperate with me. You seem very defensive. I am trying to help you.

Sith H.:
Is that why you guys beat me up? Trying to help? (laughs) Well done.

Interrogator:
You resisted arrest.

Sith H.:
No, I didn't. For fucks sake, could you at least try to lie professionally? I feel second-hand embarrassment just listening to your bullshit stories.

KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_PROP_GovSymbolOfCourage_Page_2:
Fort Narith: A Symbol of Lamang's Courage and Resolve

Nestled in the heart of Lamang’s strategic defenses, Fort Narith stands as a testament to the bravery and unwavering dedication of the Lamang Armed Forces. This formidable stronghold has become the cornerstone of our nation's efforts to safeguard sovereignty, a beacon of resilience in the face of external threats.

The soldiers stationed at Fort Narith have long been the pride of Lamang, embodying the courage and discipline that have come to define the nation’s military. They remain steadfast in their commitment to protect our land. The strength of Fort Narith lies not just in its reinforced walls, but in the indomitable spirit of those who defend it. Each soldier, from the youngest recruit to the seasoned veterans, plays a crucial role in the fort’s continued success.

We honor the defenders of Fort Narith not just for their tactical prowess, but for their unwavering dedication to the preservation of Lamang's sovereignty. Their courage serves as an inspiration to all citizens, reminding us that the strength of our nation lies in the hearts of those who are willing to sacrifice for its future.

Let us remember, as we continue to stand united, that Fort Narith represents more than just a military installation—it is a symbol of Lamang's enduring strength, and the defenders who call it home will forever be remembered as the true heroes of our land.

KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_PAP_MadmansNote2_Page_1:
It’s alive. It watches, it breathes, it knows.

I shouldn’t have gone. You think you’re just walking? HA!

Why? Why did I think I was different? Stupid. Stupid!

The ground moves. The air is thick. Shadows crawl.

I found… something. A dream? A nightmare?

The walls breathe.

Too many eyes. What do you want? What do you want?

I… I don’t remember.

The laugh. God, the laugh! Not like a person. Metal. Bone.

I left. I think I left. But it’s here. It hums.

Don’t even think about it.

I had a name.

Please.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_PAP_MadmansNote3_Page_1:
I have seen the truth! It looked at me. I shouldn't have peeked, but I did... what does that even mean? Should I have run away? But then I wouldn't be able to save her. I couldn't even do that... I should never have gone there!

I had to save my child! Was it my child? I don't know. Give him back! The truth is dissipating before my eyes! MY eyes? But I am so close! What am I, anyway?

Just a mere human who couldn't save a little girl... No, that wasn't real. But I felt it in my soul! The eons! I have witnessed so much and yet so little!

I love them so much... but who? Not real, not real, NOT REAL!

A stupid fool who doesn’t know his own place!
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_PAP_MadmansNote4_Page_1:
They're onto me!

I can feel them prowling behind me, but every time I turn around, they are not there!

Only once have I managed to catch a glimpse of them... those giant death eyes! Eyes! Eyes! EYES!!!!

Nevermore. Nevermore have I seen them again, but I can still feel them. Watching my actions. Watching my every step. Was that God? I don't know, it could have been. But what would God want from me? Cruel God!

I shouldn't have gone there. That place... it changed me. Whims...

Maybe pork stew?

I'm losing it. I know this... are you watching me, God? Answer me! I know I shouldn't desecrate that place. So heavenly beautiful, so dangerous, so otherworldly. Mother, forgive me.

Don't look at me... please...
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_PAP_MadmansNote5_Page_1:
Merciful God! I’ve seen it hovering. It was beautiful, never seen again! Nor will any other!
Eyes are a bliss given to the chosen! Am I really one of them? The child wept tears of joy...
Range is the king of happiness. Yes, the Folding King. Don't forget him and smile broadly.
Chest hurts from laughing. Why do you ask? Because the Dog is dead. Thus spoke equality.
Demons have entered our realm to the applause of the crowd. Blessed are the poor in spirit.
Onward went the lie of civilization. So little was enough to crumble into dust and despair.
Night came and went, but no one remembered that it was a change that solidified the future.
Trespassers of the heavens turned to another side, leaving scraps for the impaired to play with.
Gaia lost her virginity at the moment of truth, and no one noticed. But Existence heard the cry.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_PAP_SuicideNote_Page_1:
I can't take it anymore.

I'm losing it, but can anyone be surprised? After what we saw in the jungle? Did we really

NO, FOCUS! I have to stop thinking about it; I have managed to capture a brief moment of sanity and make the best of it.

Please, if you find this note, let my wife and child know that I love them very much, but there is no other way to end the constant nightmare that I have put myself in because of my foolish actions. 

The beholder is amused but doesn't see the big picture.

Fuck, I can't remember writing that last sentence. No more time.

I tried my best, I'm sorry.

This island is cursed.

Goodbye.

KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_LET_AuliisLetter_Page_1:
I'm sorry I wasn't strong enough. Because of my incompetence, you must have gone through hell with me. But that will change now.

By the time you read this, I will be gone. It is as it must be. What I've done, I've done for you, for the future you deserve, the life I could never give you by staying.

I know this will bring pain before it brings relief, and for that, I am sorry. If there were another way, I would have taken it. But sometimes, the only way forward is through the fire, and I chose to walk into it so you wouldn't have to.

Soon, you will have what you need. No more sleepless nights worrying about bills, no more empty fridge, no more waiting for a miracle that never comes.

Please don't waste your days questioning or resenting what had to be done. Instead, live. Spend without fear. And when you think of me, do not grieve. Just remember that I loved you enough to give you the world, even if it meant stepping out of it.

I'm selling my life at Hunter's Paradise so that you could live the life you deserve.

Be happy. That's all I ever wanted for you.

I love you.

Always yours 
Aulii

KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_ResearchNotes_Page_1:
Initial signal vector traced to RA 20h 41m / Dec +45° 16' — Deneb. That was the original assumption. In retrospect, it was only a surface reading. A projection.

Trajectory estimates from later data suggest the source was already inside the local sector when detected, possibly within 50 light-years. Movement pattern inconsistent with known astrophysical bodies.

Signal exhibits no measurable Doppler shift.

Pulse interval continues to narrow. Current average: 1.44 seconds.

No carrier degradation over time or distance.

Attempted triangulation failed - position changed without acceleration.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_ResearchNotes_Page_2:
We were wrong to call it a signal. It behaved like one, yes, but only at points of contact.

I think we only detected it when it brushed against our world. Like a ripple breaking the surface of water.

Protasov thought it was language. Pillman thought it was code. I think it was neither.

I need to copy the pages from my diary onto something else. I have to be surrounded by important days to not forget. I must not forget!
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_FL_LAFCBRNReport_Page_1:
LAF CBRN Response Unit – Situation Report

Phousai Village Assessment

At approximately 0400 hours, night-duty personnel at Fort Narith reported witnessing a brief but intense flash of blue light emanating from the direction of Phousai Village. The phenomenon lasted no more than 2–3 seconds.

Initial Findings on Site:
A unit was dispatched to ascertain the situation. Upon arrival, all civilians were confirmed deceased. Victims at the apparent epicentre had no physical remains. Only darkened silhouettes scorched into nearby walls and surfaces. Those further from the center showed signs consistent with acute radiation exposure: dermal burns, bleeding from orifices, and skin sloughing. The air had a distinct metallic taste. No mechanical sources of contamination were found. Radiation levels upon arrival were elevated but had clearly subsided since the initial event. While not immediately fatal, the residual exposure was still hazardous. A crater was located, but the blast damage was not consistent with conventional or nuclear detonation. The source of the radiation burst remains unknown.

CBRN Protocol Execution:
Cleanup began under standard procedures. Perimeter was established. Sample collection initiated. However, symptoms began manifesting among the crew within hours:

*Dizziness and nausea in two soldiers.
*One technician suffered a sudden psychotic episode - screaming and trying to extract his teeth. He was promptly subdued and transported to the infirmary.
*Others reported unease, confusion, and feelings of being followed.
*A faint humming noise was reported by multiple team members, though instruments recorded no sound.

Order to Withdraw:
Due to escalating psychological disturbances and fear of unknown contamination vectors, an order for immediate withdrawal was issued. Site was marked and sealed. Bodies were not recovered.

KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_PAP_TakeshisLetter_Page_1:
June 3, 1944.

To my dearest Hanako,

I know this letter will never reach you. None of them do. Still, I write. Because it helps me remember I am more than just a uniform.

It keeps raining every day here. Not like home. This rain is heavy and constant, falling without pause. The jungle never dries. It stinks. The air clings to your skin and the ground pulls at your feet. I hate this place.

They say we are here to protect the supply lines, but we are far from the real fighting. Just days filled with silence, then shouting, then more silence. Most of the time, we move crates. Heavy ones. My back hurts every night. Sometimes I think the war has forgotten us.

Yesterday, I buried your gift.

I am sorry, Hanako. I did not want to, but Nishida kept looking at it. He said it would be a shame to let something so beautiful hang around my neck. I laughed, but I saw the way he watched it. I could not take the risk.

There is a tree behind the southernmost bunker. Its roots curl above the ground like twisted fingers. I buried it there, between them. I hope I can dig it up before we leave. I want to carry it home and see you again.

I miss you. I will keep writing, even if the words never reach you.

Yours always,
Takeshi

Directive to Prohibit the Evacuation

Date: November 26, 2025

Fort Narith is of the utmost strategic importance to ongoing military objectives. It has been determined that the presence of local civilians in their villages is essential to maintaining stability, minimizing disruption, and supporting broader mission objectives.

By order of the Commander:

Effective immediately, all personnel are directed to take necessary measures to prevent the evacuation of local villagers from their homes and surrounding areas (Savanpha village and Nakasa village).

Prohibited Actions:

        * Facilitating, organizing, or directly participating in the evacuation of local villagers.

        * Providing transportation, logistical support, or safe passage for villagers attempting to leave the designated area.

Authorized Actions:

        * Monitor and secure village perimeters to ensure compliance with this order.

        * Work with local leaders to communicate the need to remain in their villages.

        * Providing humanitarian assistance and reassurance to support the needs of the local population.

        * Preventing the evacuation of villagers by lethal force, if necessary.

All personnel must strictly adhere to this directive. Any deviation or unauthorized action that facilitates the evacuation of villagers will result in disciplinary action.

Officers at all levels are required to submit regular status reports on compliance with this order. Any incidents involving attempts to evacuate villagers must be reported immediately to the Fort Narith Commander.

This order will remain in effect until explicitly rescinded by the Fort Narith Commander or Commander-in-Chief Narith Sayavong.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_FL_FNCommanderInvestigation_Pag:
Confidential Army Report

Date: July 5, 2025

Subject: Investigation of Unauthorized Actions by Fort Narith Commander

Executive Summary

This report outlines the findings of an investigation into the actions of the Fort Narith Commander, who was found to have engaged in unauthorized activities, including the sale of weapons without proper authorization and the procurement of jets unsuitable for military use.

Background

Commander Nuon Bintang has served in the LAF for many years. The allegations arose following a routine audit.
The investigation was initiated to determine the extent of the alleged misconduct, its impact, and the appropriate disciplinary action.

Findings

* Unauthorized Sale of Weapons
* Procurement of Unsuitable Jets

Commander Nuon Bintang was also involved in acquiring new jets deemed inappropriate for the operational needs of the LAF. Issues identified include:

* Technical deficiencies: The jets failed to meet specific operational criteria (combat readiness standards).
* Financial loss: The purchase resulted in a financial loss of approximately $60,000,000.

Recommendations

The following actions are recommended to address the situation and prevent future occurrences:

        * Immediate disciplinary action: Suspend Commander Nuon Bintang from duty pending further legal or administrative proceedings.
        * Recovery measures: Initiate efforts to trace and recover the weapons sold and address the financial implications of the unsuitable jets.
        * Training and awareness: Implement mandatory training on ethical decision-making and adherence to acquisition protocols.
        * Enhanced oversight: Establish a robust auditing and monitoring system to detect and deter similar activities.

Conclusion

Commander Nuon Bintang's actions seriously violate military regulations and ethical standards. Prompt and decisive action must be taken to mitigate the consequences, hold the individual accountable, and restore trust in the LAF.

KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_FL_UNPreCrisisAnalysis_Page_1:
PRE-CRISIS IN THE DEMOCRATIC REPUBLIC OF LAMANG

May 17, 2025

1. OVERVIEW:

Recent assessments conducted by the UN, in coordination with partner agencies and satellite logistics teams, suggest a high likelihood of a rapid-onset humanitarian emergency within The Democratic Republic of Lamang in the coming months. While official government channels continue to downplay early warning indicators, on-the-ground conditions reflect systemic vulnerability and a critical lack of preparedness.


2. FIELD OBSERVATIONS:

* Health infrastructure remains under-resourced; rural districts lack basic emergency response capacity.

* Government stockpiles are reportedly overstated; actual distribution mechanisms are either untested or defunct.

* The civilian population shows no visible signs of awareness campaigns or contingency protocols.

* Communication with local authorities has been inconsistent, with responses alternating between dismissive and non-committal.

3. RISK ANALYSIS:

Indicators point toward a convergence of environmental strain, economic fragility, and sociopolitical tension—any of which could independently trigger a crisis. Combined, they present a credible threat of nationwide destabilization by the end of the year at the latest.

Failure to act preemptively will result in reactive logistics, bottlenecked aid entry, and preventable civilian loss.


4. RECOMMENDATIONS:

* Deployment of UNLRA personnel.

* Pre-positioning of critical relief supplies in adjacent logistics hubs.

* Engagement with non-governmental and neutral civil organizations within the country.

KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_FL_EventAffectedPopulation_Page:
Classified

Date: December 15, 2025

Subject: Event - Affected Population

Introduction

This report analyzes the physiological and psychological effects observed in individuals exposed to the Event. Data has been compiled from extensive medical records and classified research files, including findings from studies conducted by the UNLRA. The observations indicate severe and unprecedented biological transformations requiring further investigation.

Findings

        Complete Cellular Degradation

        Medical examinations reveal total cellular breakdown in affected individuals. The degradation process appears to be accelerated and irreversible, leading to progressive organ failure. Notably, tissue samples exhibit a loss of structural integrity at an advanced rate, suggesting a fundamental change at the cellular level.

        Profound Molecular Alterations

        Subjects display significant molecular modifications, which include:

   *Unusual protein synthesis patterns
   *Mutations in DNA sequences
   *Disruptions in normal metabolic pathways. These alterations contribute to abnormal physiological responses, including heightened regenerative properties in some cases, while others suffer from rapid necrotic decay.

Psychological Manifestations

        A strong correlation has been observed between exposure to the Event and the onset of severe psychiatric disorders, including:

   *Acute psychosis
   *Hallucinations (both auditory and visual)
   *Paranoia and heightened aggression
   *Cognitive dissonance and memory fragmentation. The extent of these symptoms varies, but in several cases, they have led to complete mental deterioration.

Conclusion

The implications of these findings are profound. The data confirms that the Event has caused irreversible biological and psychological damage to those affected. Given the severity of the symptoms and transformations, further research is necessary to determine potential containment or treatment strategies. The UN's role in researching and experimenting on affected individuals has yielded valuable yet ethically complex information. Immediate action is required to assess long-term consequences and potential countermeasures.

Relevant medical findings are attached to this report.
