KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_TheresNoReasonToPanic_Page:
There's no reason to panic

November 26, 2025

"Undoubtedly, an unwarranted panic is spreading throughout our society. Lies and delusions have infected the minds of our fellow citizens and are eating away at the community we have so long built together. Do not be fooled by rumors that have no basis in reality. Our country has never been better."

These are the words of Vice President Jan Park, and we have to agree. As journalists with a duty to present truthful information and news to our fellow citizens, we are compelled to make a similar statement to our readers. There is no indication that anything unusual is happening. We are one of the safest countries in the world, and no one will divide our community.


Obey All Orders from the Armed Forces

There have been recent incidents where civilians failed to comply with instructions from police and military personnel. We remind all citizens that order must be maintained, especially during these exceptional circumstances.

Respect the law. Cooperate fully. This situation is temporary, and with unity and discipline, normal life will resume soon.


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

My fellow citizens — can’t you see what’s happening right before your eyes? Isn’t it obvious that everything we’ve witnessed is part of a psychological war waged by foreign intelligence agencies?

They’ve tried everything: lies, slander, persecution. But they haven’t broken us. We are strong. We are united.

Do not let the colonizers — those parasites — tear apart our beautiful homeland. They want us gone. They send their lackeys, the so-called United Nations Lamang Relief Agency, to do their dirty work. And when your homes are abandoned, they’ll move in and call upon their armies to claim whatever is left.

Make no mistake: the West is eager to colonize us once again.

DO NOT BELIEVE THEIR LIES!

Film Review by Nok Dam — Wicked: For Good

This film is as dreadful as the situation Lamang now faces under the weight of Western oppression. The first installment was already a slog — forgettable songs and shallow, millennial-grade writing — and the sequel offers nothing but more of the same.

Curse the creators of this mess. I was forced to sit through it during one of the darkest chapters in our island’s history.

1/10


Fun with Girls

Why did the scarecrow win an award? Because he was outstanding in his field.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_TheWorldIsWatching_Page_1:
THE WORLD IS WATCHING

January 15, 2025

The brilliant vision of our glorious President Narith has put our island on the world map! His grand plans are coming to fruition as foreign newspapers discuss the beautiful Midnight Sapphire Resort. But enough of my writing; here is the praise from the Worldview News.

"The number one tourist destination for wealthy individuals has become the Midnight Sapphire Resort in the Democratic Republic of Lamang. This small country in Southeast Asia is making big waves lately as many Hollywood stars praise the generosity of the locals and the modern approach to hospitality service they have experienced."

It's wonderful to read such warm words from our foreign colleagues. We are sure there will be more similar articles about our beautiful homeland to share with our dear readers.


Scientific Breakthrough in National Productivity

Recent findings from our nation's top scientists have uncovered a remarkable, some might even say sacred, phenomenon. Every time our esteemed President, Narith Sayavong, visits a workplace, there is a statistically significant boost in productivity.

"This is indeed the case," confirmed Professor Phai Lor. "We've dubbed it the Sayavong Effect, and we're currently exploring methods to harness this phenomenon on a national scale."


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

Our land is a paradise.

You might have your doubts; maybe you've seen a glossy Hollywood film or some polished foreign promo, but believe me, there's no place like Lamang.

I've traveled the world. I've seen the skyscrapers of New York, wandered through Russia, sipped coffee beneath the Eiffel Tower, eaten sushi in Okinawa, and even braved the dreariness of London… But none of those places come close to the richness of our culture.

There's something truly special here, something you only begin to understand once you leave. And that's precisely why I urge you: don't go! Don't waste your time. Out there, it's a barren land.

There's a reason so many Lamangese return after chasing illusions abroad.

And above all, we have universal healthcare! That alone speaks volumes.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_TheWorldIsWatching_Page_2:
Film Review by Nok Dam — Kadhalikka Neramillai

Kadhalikka Neramillai is a charming Indian romantic comedy written and directed by Kiruthiga Udhayanidhi. The film explores themes of modern relationships, single parenthood, and societal expectations, with a focus on how differing views on family and relationships evolve.

Even though the second part is a little weaker, the film still feels like a breath of fresh air compared to many formulaic Hollywood rom-coms. Definitely give it a chance. It’s entertaining and, in many ways, relatable. After all, like us, Indians have also endured the cruelty of the British Empire. In spirit, they’re like cousins.

7/10


Fun with Girls

Never confide in a vacuum cleaner. They’re always collecting dirt.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_MidnightSapphireMiracle_Pa:
The Midnight Sapphire Miracle

March 11, 2009

Hundreds of our fellow citizens are volunteering to be evicted from their homes as they understand the imposing vision of our wise President Narith Sayavong.

"When I heard that our President has such grand plans for the place where my family and I have lived for generations, I started to cry with pride. It is an honor to make way for such a project," said one of the residents.

Construction of the Midnight Sapphire Resort has already begun, creating new jobs for many Lamangese natives.

"I am excited that the entire Lamang nation shares my vision for the future of our country," Narith Sayavong commented. "Together, and only together, we will build a paradise on earth."

A marvelous statement from our beloved leader. We wish all local citizens the best of luck in finding a new home through the relocation program and look forward to the completion of the magnificent Midnight Sapphire.


A Stump Was on Fire in Nam Thaven

Shortly before 1 p.m. on Tuesday, firefighters went to the outskirts of Nam Thaven. A stump was on fire, so they extinguished it and returned to the base. Authorities confirm that the situation was handled promptly and there is no threat to the public.


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

At times, I feel almost ashamed of how much our beloved state does for us. Have I truly earned these privileges? Have I done enough to deserve the care and support I receive?

I know I’m not alone in these thoughts. Many of you must feel the same. Our state nurtures us, gives us purpose, educates us, and guides us through life, like devoted parents who are always there and always dependable.

It shocks me to see how the West treats its citizens. A society ruled by brute power, tearing itself apart like a wounded beast. Millions sleep on cold streets while the Western states look away, indifferent in their apathy. It’s horrifying.

Here in Lamang, we are truly fortunate. So, instead of asking what more the state can do for us, let’s ask ourselves what we can do for it. Let’s give back the love, care, and stability it has so generously given us.

Film Review by Nok Dam — Watchmen

Watchmen is a comic book adaptation that explores the personal struggles and deep flaws of its so-called superheroes as they navigate their complicated lives.

It is an exceptional adaptation with phenomenal actors. The scenes with Rorschach still give me chills.

If you're looking for a movie that dives into tough moral choices and the blurry line between right and wrong, this one’s a must-watch. The biggest downfall of this movie is that the author of the comics, Alan Moore, is British.

9/10


Fun with Girls

A horse walks into a bar and the bartender says: "Oh my God! Somebody call animal services!"
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_SuburbsControversy_Page_1:
Suburbs Controversy

October 20, 2019

Rumors have been circulating about a new real estate development project on the outskirts of the city. Many locals have spoken out, raising concerns about potential harm to the environment and suggesting the development caters exclusively to wealthy individuals looking to isolate themselves from the broader community.

We reached out to the mayor for comment.

"I want to reassure everyone that the project will comply fully with the law, and that the natural surroundings will be protected," the mayor said. "Claims that these new buildings are only for the upper class are simply untrue. This development will create a wide range of opportunities, including essential services and jobs that will benefit the entire community."

As promised, we looked into the issue. With the mayor's statement, concerned residents can feel confident that the project is being handled with care and consideration. We're glad to have brought some clarity to the conversation.


Tragedy at Tonmai Hainy Lumber Company

A terrible tragedy occurred at the Tonmain Hainy Lumber Company when a falling log struck and killed a worker instantly.

In response to circulating rumors, a company spokesperson stated: “We want to reassure the public that our company is not cursed, despite recent speculation. While this marks the third fatal accident in recent years, these incidents are not the result of anything supernatural. Unfortunately, they stem from workers disregarding proper safety procedures.”


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

From time to time, the state must clear parts of our beautiful wilderness to make way for new projects. While this may seem unfortunate, it is a necessary step made with careful thought and purpose. Trust that the government understands what is needed to build a better future.

Every such project is backed by extensive planning, analysis, and documentation to ensure it brings lasting value to our nation. What might appear today as the loss of heritage is, in truth, a transformation, an investment in what lies ahead. What belonged to yesterday may no longer serve the needs of tomorrow.

So let us not mourn what is passing. Instead, let us look forward with a spirit of progress and renewal, knowing that the benefits of today’s decisions will shape a stronger, brighter future for us all.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_SuburbsControversy_Page_2:
Film Review by Nok Dam — The Lighthouse

Robert Pattinson and Willem Dafoe deliver outstanding performances as lighthouse keepers who are slowly losing their grip on reality. Director Robert Eggers creates a surreal, claustrophobic story filled with myth, psychological dread, and farting.

Overall, it's an intriguing exploration of the human psyche and the encounter with the unknown.

8/10


Fun with Girls

Why should you never play poker at the zoo? Too many cheetahs.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_SlaversShutDown_Page_1:
Slavers Shut Down

December 2, 2024

Police units carried out a successful raid targeting organized crime near the Blue Lagoon earlier this week. The infamous Fanny Paradise strip club was shut down, and its owner was taken into custody after authorities uncovered evidence of human trafficking and forced labor.

“We will not stand for these kinds of practices in Lamang,” said Vice President Jan Park. “Let this serve as a clear message: those who exploit others will be brought to justice.”

Moments like this remind us of the strength of our institutions. Lamang remains a country committed to safety, justice, and the values of a true democracy.


President Narith Sayavong Visits Sunset Chalet

President Narith Sayavong, accompanied by Vice President Jan Park, paid a visit to Sunset Chalet near the Midnight Sapphire Resort, where they admired the breathtaking scenery and enjoyed a cup of coffee.

“It was truly a life-changing experience to have the honor of serving our great leader, President Narith Sayavong,” said Khempheng Thammavong, a waitress at Sunset Chalet. “I will cherish this memory for the rest of my life. I feel truly blessed.”


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

Our nation is built on cooperation and mutual trust. That’s why it’s always disheartening to hear of individuals who seek to undermine that foundation. What took place in that infamous strip club — whose name I won’t even dignify by mentioning — stands in direct opposition to the values our country upholds.

Even in the most tolerant societies, a few rotten apples will emerge. Fortunately, we have the means to prevent disgraceful acts like this from spreading. If you witness anything similar in your community, do not hesitate — report it to the police or government authorities.

Let it be known: crime has no place in Lamang.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_SlaversShutDown_Page_2:
Film Review by Nok Dam — Wicked

I was seriously tempted to walk out of the theater, but I managed to hold it together. It's part of the job. On the other hand, you don't need to suffer like I did, so do yourself a favor and skip this soulless mess.

Not a single song sticks, not even by accident. More and more, it feels like the West is trying to erode our culture through its entertainment industry. Wicked isn't just bad; it's a weaponized form of boredom, right up there with The Boy and the Heron.

1/10


Fun with Girls

Have you heard the joke about yoga? Never mind, it's a bit of a stretch.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_MysteriousLights_Page_1:
Mysterious Lights

August 17, 2020

Strange lights have been spotted around Ban Pa, a remote village on the eastern side of the island long wrapped in mystery. Eyewitnesses claim to have seen ghostly figures and other supernatural phenomena in the area, adding to the region's eerie reputation.

“We’re not entirely sure what people may have witnessed,” said Vice President Jan Park. “But we strongly advise both citizens and tourists to stay away from Ban Pa and its surroundings. The local residents are known to be unfriendly toward outsiders, and the area remains hazardous due to abandoned Japanese-era structures—possibly even old land mines and unexploded ordnance.”

Ban Pa has become something of a legend in our nation, and its secrets continue to spark curiosity. However, people should really avoid this place as much as they can.


Twelve Hours of Horror and Despair

A terrifying incident occurred near Kiu Vongsa, where an elderly woman became trapped under a tree root after a fall. In shock and completely exhausted, she lost her voice and was unable to call for help, even as people searched for her nearby.

“It was horrible… I was there for twelve hours,” said Nang Lee, her voice still shaking.

Thankfully, the ordeal ended on a positive note. Nang was eventually found and rescued, suffering only minor bruises and a few scratches.


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

Many myths circulate throughout our nation — tales of ancient idols, vengeful spirits, monsters lurking in the woods, and long-lost treasures. We're not alone in this; much of Southeast Asia shares a rich tradition of similar folklore.

They say every legend holds a sliver of truth, and perhaps that's true. But more often than not, these supernatural tales have down-to-earth explanations. People lost in the deep jungle may have fallen victim to bandits. The traveler who survived the fall wasn't saved by a forest spirit but rather by plain luck. And the ghosts are often nothing more than projections of a guilty conscience.

Storytelling is a deeply human instinct. Today, we invent new myths, for example, about aliens watching from the skies or hidden Japanese storage caves — modern echoes of old tales about gods and treasures.

Imagination knows no bounds, and sometimes it veers into the absurd. People will even spread horrifying stories about our fair and just state, just because they were fined for speeding.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_MysteriousLights_Page_2:
Film Review by Nok Dam — Wonder Woman 1984

Wonder Woman 1984 is a colorful spectacle, but nothing more. Gal Gadot attempts to embody a mythic heroine, yet her character spends more time in shopping malls than facing meaningful conflict. The film lacks any real struggle unless we count the viewer’s struggle to finish watching it.

The first Wonder Woman was better.

4/10


Fun with Girls

Why are frogs always so happy? They eat whatever bugs them.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_ParadiseFound_Page_1:
Paradise Found

November 14, 2025

In a recent interview, Lamang’s Vice President Jan Park addressed international critics who have likened our nation to North Korea. Park dismissed the comparisons as unfounded and invited the world to experience the truth for themselves.

"I don’t understand why Lamang is being compared to a closed-off country like North Korea," Park remarked. "We are not a nation of secrecy or isolation. We are a land of open arms and warm smiles. Anyone who visits will see for themselves that Lamang is closer to paradise on earth than anywhere else in the world."

It's hard to argue with Park's claim. Lamang boasts an abundance of breathtaking tourist attractions that rival the world's most famous destinations. For example, the wildly beautiful Blue Lagoon offers a serene escape for nature lovers. The luxurious Midnight Sapphire Resort provides unparalleled luxury and hospitality, and for those seeking a mix of culture and coastal adventure, Tiger Bay is a hub of vibrant markets and charming seaside cafes.

Indeed, Lamang is far from the dreary caricature some might imagine. For travelers seeking beauty, tranquility, and authentic Southeast Asian charm, Lamang is not only worth a visit—it’s a must-see.


Lamang Celebrates!

Farmers across Lamang are rejoicing after an unusually successful rubber harvest this season. Favorable weather conditions and improved tapping techniques contributed to a significant increase in latex yield, with some regions reporting their best results in over a decade. This achievement reflects the strength of Lamang’s agricultural policy and the resilience of its people.


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

It’s honestly disheartening to see Western countries label our free and proud nation as a dictatorship. For centuries, they exploited us — and now they preach from some imaginary moral high ground? The West is bombing countries across the globe, yet we’re painted as the villains?

Thankfully, not everyone in the West is blind to the double standards their governments push on the world. Just recently, I spoke with a couple of Americans at Hunter’s Paradise — a wonderful place, by the way; you should visit if you get the chance — and they were genuinely ashamed of how Lamang is portrayed back home.

I’m truly grateful for people like them. They give me hope that the West can still move beyond its post-colonial mindset.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_ParadiseFound_Page_2:
Film Review by Nok Dam — The Running Man

Edgar Wright’s The Running Man is a gripping reboot, with a streetwise visual style and emotional core. Glen Powell may not have the raw charisma or acting chops of Arnold Schwarzenegger, but he delivers a solid performance that grounds the story.

Wright once again proves his remarkable talent for adaptation — as he did with Scott Pilgrim vs. the World — and this film only reinforces that reputation.

It’s just a pity he’s British.

8/10


Fun with Girls

My grandfather has the heart of a lion and a lifetime ban from the zoo.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_RaksmeiSayavong_Page_1:
Raksmei Sayavong (1924-1996)

June 23, 1996

It is with great sorrow that we must announce the death of our beloved leader and the father of our free nation.

He tragically lost his life in an automobile accident, and with him, the world has lost a towering figure who helped shape modern history.

Let us honor the sacrifices he made in the name of freedom. As a young man, he was obliged to leave his family and join the freedom fighters who stood bravely against a criminal regime that took power after the expulsion of the vicious British.

In 1968, his lifelong dream became reality when he led the liberation of the Lamang people. Under his leadership, the Sons of Lamang fulfilled their mission and established a legitimate democratic government. That same year, fortune smiled on him once more with the birth of his son, Narith, who now, with a heavy heart, takes up the mantle to lead our nation into a new millennium.

His legacy will be remembered with deep admiration for generations to come.

Farewell, beloved liberator.


National Mourning

From every corner of Lamang, the people's cries and sorrow can be heard throughout the land. Citizens are in disbelief at the immense loss our nation has suffered.

Work has come to a standstill as the workers cannot steady their trembling hands, and offices remain closed as tears stream down the faces of those inside.

The entire nation mourns the passing of our leader, our hero, our liberator.


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

The heart of our great leader, burning with unwavering love for his people, has ceased to beat. This is the greatest tragedy in the nation's history.

As I write these words, the emptiness is beyond expression. Our beloved President, Raksmei Sayavong, has left us forever. It feels inconceivable.

In the streets, people wail in despair, tearing their clothes in sorrow. Even our strongest soldiers weep openly, unable to contain the grief that grips the nation.

What shall we do without our liberator? The man who led the Sons of Lamang to glorious victory over a corrupt regime... who rewrote our destiny and left an indelible mark on the world.

Even the heavens weep for the fallen President, the man who brought eternal glory to the Lamangese people.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_RaksmeiSayavong_Page_2:
Comment by Nok Dam

There can be no film review when the nation is in mourning over the tragic loss of our beloved liberator.

Gone is the hero who struck down the corrupt regime that conspired with foreign powers.

Gone is the President who loved his people fiercely and dared to stand against the mightiest forces in the world.

Gone is the man who changed his destiny, and ours too.

We will never forget you, President Raksmei Sayavong. Thank you for everything.


Raksmei Sayavong Quote:

“Our nation has a responsibility to stand as a shining example of an independent and resilient small country, inspiring others like us to break free from Western imperialist domination.

Stand firm, Lamang.”
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_UnitedNationsMission_Page_:
United Nations Mission

July 13, 2025

Today, the United Nations arrived in our homeland.

To avoid any confusion, we are publishing the official statement from President Narith Sayavong regarding the UN’s decision to launch an operation in Lamang:

“Many of you may be afraid, thinking the UN has invaded our homeland — but rest assured, they are here with my full approval.

For too long, our nation has been slandered on the world stage as a so-called dictatorship that oppresses its people. I know that’s not true. You know that’s not true. And now, the whole world will see the truth for itself.

If anyone feels their voice has not been heard, you are welcome to speak with UN representatives — known here as the UNLRA. But let me make one thing clear: I am always here to listen.

No one else will help us — only Lamangese people can truly help one another.”

We are proud to publish this statement from our President and urge all citizens to remain calm during the arrival of the UNLRA. The United Nations will soon see for themselves that the accusations against us were false — and that we have nothing to hide.


Lowest Crime Rate

The Democratic Republic of Lamang is experiencing a sixth consecutive year of plummeting crime statistics.

“We are doing a good job educating the people that crime does not pay,” police spokesman Sao Yang commented.


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

We have always upheld transparency in our nation. There are no hidden truths to uncover. That is precisely why we allowed the presence of UN forces. Their mission is to witness firsthand that Lamang is a peaceful and modern democracy.

We trust that the UNLRA will respect our laws, customs, and sovereignty and refrain from attempting to impose a new form of colonialism. The people of Lamang are peaceful by nature, but our tolerance should never be mistaken for submission.

If they set aside their degenerate Western habits and choose to live in harmony with us, they will be met with open arms. But if they arrive bearing the whip, they will learn that Lamang is not to be trifled with.

Film Review by Nok Dam — Jurassic World: Rebirth

Another Jurassic something. Hollywood continues to squeeze every last drop from this franchise as if their survival depended on it.

Once again, highly trained mercenaries are inexplicably useless, while a dysfunctional American family is the one group that manages to outwit prehistoric monsters.

The overall plot feels basic, and the only thing saving this movie from the swamp of mediocrity is Scarlett Johansson.

6/10


Fun with Girls

What does a spy do when he is cold? He goes undercover.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_OpeningOfFortRaksmei_Page_:
Opening of Fort Raksmei

May 2, 1975

A new symbol of unbreakable revolutionary strength has risen on the sacred soil of the homeland. Under the extraordinary guidance of President Raksmei Sayavong, the inauguration ceremony of a newly constructed modern fort was held yesterday with great revolutionary fervor.

This impenetrable fortress stands as a bulwark of our military might, a shining testament to the outstanding achievements of President Raksmei Sayavong, who continues to lead the Lamang people on the path of self-reliance, defense, and victory.

In a congratulatory message, President Raksmei Sayavong praised the patriotic devotion of the soldiers, engineers, workers, and USSR consultants who labored tirelessly to complete this historic project ahead of schedule.


End of a Traitor

In the early hours of the morning, General Sangha Vang was executed by hanging for crimes of treason.

"I am deeply ashamed of the actions I took. My moral weakness brought loss and sorrow upon my country," the General said in his final statement. "I pray that my death may wash the shame that has befallen my family."

Following his execution, the former General’s widow and two daughters were transferred to the Homphu Suk labor camp for political reeducation.


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

To be truly free, a nation must first be strong. The world does not respect those who cannot defend themselves. That is why it is essential that we strengthen our military by any means necessary.

We must be relentless in our mission to turn our island into an impenetrable fortress — one that no imperialist power would dare to challenge. The construction of Fort Raksmei is a vital step toward that vision. A modern military base of this scale will send a clear message: we are not to be underestimated.

Yet an army is only as strong as the people within it. And if we cannot trust our defenders, how can they protect us? The case of General Sangha Vang has revealed a hard truth: even in our new and thriving society, corruption and betrayal can still take root. For some, no amount of prosperity is ever enough.

We must remain vigilant against such opportunists who would trade their honor for thirty pieces of silver. Our future depends on it.

Film Review by Nok Kao — Death Race 2000

Death Race 2000 is a grim reflection of capitalist decadence disguised as entertainment. The story is set in a dystopian future where pedestrians are run over for points and the amusement of the viewers.

Though advertised as satire, any discerning viewer will recognize that this is less a parody and more a reflection of where the imperialist United States is headed. A nation obsessed with conflict, spectacle, and manufactured drama is bound to produce something similar to what is depicted in this disgusting film.

4/10


Lonely Hearts

Marriage Proposal

A 32-year-old widower with stable employment in public service seeks a lifelong companion. Due to limited personal time, I am pursuing this path to find a sincere and lasting relationship.

Only genuine inquiries should be directed to the administration of The Voice of Lamang, labeled under "Serious Relationship."
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_NeverAgain_Page_1:
Never Again!

February 14, 2024

Today marks exactly 200 years since one of the darkest chapters in Lamang’s history — when our homeland was seized and turned into a British colony.

At the time, we did not fully understand what was happening. We were unwillingly forced into servitude by foreign rulers from a distant island, thousands of kilometers away, who claimed the right to decide our fate.

It took many years — and countless sacrifices — before we broke free and reclaimed our sovereignty. But every struggle was worth it when we look at the Lamang of today, and all we have achieved under the leadership of President Narith Sayavong.

“We are a people who cherish peace and stability. But we will never again allow our country to be enslaved. We will defend it with whatever we have — and if it comes to it, we will fight with our fists and our teeth,” said Vice President Jan Park.

We fully stand by his words. Should our sovereignty ever again be threatened, there is no doubt that the people of Lamang will rise as one to defend our nation from any would-be usurpers. Let history be our witness: Lamang will never kneel again!


Death to the Enemies of Liberty

Across towns and villages throughout the nation, the people of Lamang raise their voices in pride as the Union Jack burns.

“We were meant to stay on standby today,” said Malaithong Sisoulith, chief of the eastern fire department, “but when we saw the British flags set ablaze, we couldn’t help but join in.”

Everyone is doing their part.


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

I’m deeply proud to see our country steadily moving away from its colonial legacy. Today, many ordinary citizens can hardly speak English anymore — and that’s a remarkable achievement. It took major education reforms and a great deal of sacrifice, but we’ve done it. We’ve returned to our roots. I feel a surge of pride when I walk through our cities and hear, almost exclusively, our native language instead of the tongue of our former oppressors. On this sombre anniversary, it’s significant that this linguistic shift is now nearly complete.

To my fellow citizens: do not let yourselves be lured back into the trap of the English language. Let the burden of learning and using that cursed tongue fall to politicians, scientists, businesspeople, and their families. You should take joy in speaking your mother tongue freely and proudly.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_NeverAgain_Page_2:
Film Review by Nok Dam — Madame Web

Madame Web is a muddled Marvel spinoff that struggles under a convoluted script, uninspired direction, and a full-blown tonal identity crisis. While Dakota Johnson delivers occasional moments of muted charm, the film is bogged down by heavy exposition, sloppy effects, and a lack of coherent vision.

This movie serves as yet another reminder that, for all their dazzling technology, Western imperialists remain ideologically bankrupt. As such, it is but another shiny distraction from a culture in decline.

2/10


Fun with Girls

The sun never set on the British Empire because even God didn’t trust them in the dark.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_ThreeHundredthAnn_Page_1:
The Three Hundredth Anniversary

October 12, 1993

Like many nations across the globe, we too were “discovered” by the wretched Westerners in the 17th century. We remember with hatred the times when the British and the Japanese occupied our island. But let us never forget, it wasn’t these empires who first stained our sacred soil. No, the first usurpers came from yet another European nation. But I’ll say no more. Let us now hear the words of our liberator, President Raksmei Sayavong:

“Three hundred years ago, a Dutch captain named Adriaen Brunel arrived on our shores. He was in charge of a trading ship called the Oosterse Bloem, which sought supplies, food, and fresh water for its crew. We welcomed him. We offered to help, as we have always assisted those in need. And that was our mistake. In the years that followed, the Westerners returned again and again, taking whatever they pleased. Our water, our food, and even our women. This did not stop, including during British rule. We were reduced to slaves. Don't ever forgive the British for what they did to us. But keep a small part of your anger for the Dutch, for it was they who first brought the Western cruelty to our land.”


Man Steals Chicken and Locks Himself in a Toilet; Only Bones Were Left

A hungry 28-year-old man, without any money, visited Nam Thaven in search of a proper snack. He seized a grilled chicken and locked himself in a toilet, refusing to open the door to authorities until he had finished eating.

When the door was finally opened, only the bones remained.


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

For much of our recent history, we were nothing more than a British colony, a minuscule dot on their world map. They held us hostage on our own land, working us to the bone.

Past events have shown that looking elsewhere brought no comfort. The Dutch had been robbing our resources even before the British arrived. And when our fellow Asians, the Japanese, came, they treated us no better. To them, we were just cheap labor.

We once had a strong relationship with the Soviets, but sadly, they too have fallen.

And now? Now we watch the world with caution, sensing that the age of new empires is upon us.

There is only one nation we can genuinely trust, and that is ourselves: the Lamangese.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_ThreeHundredthAnn_Page_2:
Film Review by Nok Dam — Demolition Man

Demolition Man offers a dystopian glimpse into a future where Western liberalism has spiraled into absurdity. Beneath the false promises of peace and order, citizens have had their freedoms systematically stripped away.

Sylvester Stallone stars as the rugged enforcer of the past, facing off against a hyper-violent Wesley Snipes. Both deliver standout performances.

The film is a brilliant, entertaining look at the fate that may await the imperialist world. Director Marco Brambilla shows immense talent, and I’m genuinely excited to see what he does next.

9/10


Fun with Girls

What did the janitor say when he jumped out of the closet? Supplies!
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_TrialOfNationsTraitors_Pag:
Trial of the Nation's Traitors

December 5, 1968

Yesterday, twelve former government officials were found guilty of high treason and sentenced to death by hanging. These individuals, who had clearly served British imperialist interests over those of our own people, faced the full weight of justice. The people of Lamang are angry and disgusted by their crimes. The State Prosecutor's Office has received hundreds of resolutions, including those from factories and offices. "We demand the strictest possible punishment for the troublemakers," write the lumberjacks and other workers of the old timber factories. Other resolutions, equally angry, but reflecting the firm belief of our people in maintaining lasting peace, are addressed to those who have broken all international laws most shamefully and have launched a campaign against our young democracy.

"In the name of the people of Lamang, let the sentence fall like an iron fist — without an ounce of mercy!" declared the presiding judge in a powerful closing statement. He emphasized that in the new democratic country, those who betray their homeland will face swift and uncompromising punishment. The citizens of Lamang welcomed the verdict with overwhelming approval.

We will not allow the Republic to fall apart!

Long live democracy!


General Joy

With revolutionary fervor, songs of joy and celebration echo across Lamang.

“Long live the Republic! Long live our beloved President Raksmei Sayavong!” cry the peasants and workers in unison.

The dark shadows of  our colonial legacy have been cast away. At last, the citizens of Lamang can breathe freely in the light of true independence!


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

I was born in the jungle. My earliest memory is of my family fleeing from the ruthless forces of the traitors who seized our homeland and violated it.

I curse them. Because of them, I never had a childhood. From the very beginning, I was a warrior… and a dreamer. I studied grand political theories from a young age — not for leisure, but out of necessity. I had to defend my ideals and spread the truth among the oppressed people of Lamang.

The old regime fostered ignorance and illiteracy, yet had the audacity to call us barbarians. While my peers toiled in rice paddies to feed pompous officials in their villas, I devoured the works of Marx, Nietzsche, and Sartre by the age of seven.

But now, a new chapter begins. It's time to purge the rot — those who tried to keep us buried in the mud. We will destroy them as we destroy illiteracy, poverty, and nepotism. This is the dawn of a new age, an age of opportunity for every citizen of Lamang.

You needn't look far for proof. Look at me. When before was it ever possible for a jungle-born, despised by the old world, to become a journalist… at just fifteen?

Film Review by Nok Kao — Planet of the Apes

Planet of the Apes is a revolutionary movie that blends science fiction themes with sharp social commentary. The story is about a cosmonaut stranded in a world ruled by intelligent apes, where humans have been reduced to primitive creatures. I won't say more to avoid spoiling the brilliant story and its unexpected ending.

The makeup effects are gorgeous, and the script is rich with thought-provoking ideas. If you get the chance to visit the cinema, don't hesitate.

9/10


Lonely Hearts

Businessman, single, handsome

Fifty-five years old, tall, and wealthy. Due to a lack of time and opportunity, seeks a sympathetic, pretty girl or a lady with some dowry. Recommendations from parents, relatives, or acquaintances are welcome.

Mark: Discretion guaranteed.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_InsurrectionAverted_Page_1:
Insurrection Averted!

September 22, 2002

The British imperialists have never forgiven us for driving them out of our homeland. Their bitterness endures, and even decades later, they continue to meddle in our affairs, hoping to corrupt the good citizens of Lamang. Sadly, not all among us have the strength to resist the seductive pull of imperialist gold.

What began as a seemingly routine strike by timber workers in the island's eastern region quickly escalated into a full-blown rebellion. Fueled by Western influence and greed, the traitorous rebels turned their weapons against riot police, spilling the blood of their fellow countrymen.

Leading this treacherous uprising was fraudster Lani Sayavong, who has long benefited from the undeserved prestige of sharing a surname with our revered leaders. But his days of impunity are over. Thanks to the swift and courageous action of young commander Nuon Bintang, the rebellion was crushed in its infancy. Though a handful of rebels fled into the dense jungle, it won't be long before they are all caught and brought to justice, where they will receive a fair, yet merciless trial.


Young Love Drama

A teenage girl from Tiger Bay was arrested after she reportedly threatened her boyfriend with a kitchen knife.

"She's crazy," said the young man, now her ex. "I wouldn't be surprised if she actually murdered someone one day."

After spending the night in a police station, she was sent back to her parents. Authorities hope that this experience will serve as a wake-up call.


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

Betrayal — there's no word more chilling.

It wears many faces, but none more vile than treason. Treason against one's homeland. What kind of creature would sell itself to foreign powers and cast aside everything we've built together? Who, in their right mind, would willingly tear down their own beautiful country?

Disagreement has its place. Debate is part of a healthy society. But the moment innocent lives are taken, a line is crossed. Unforgivable. These are not revolutionaries. They are terrorists. Mindless, destructive terrorists.

We must root every last one out before this disease spreads. These parasites hiding among us cannot be ignored. It is your duty, as citizens, to act. Report these traitors, these wicked contra-revolutionaries. Help our nation get rid of these cockroaches before they drag you down with them.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_InsurrectionAverted_Page_2:
Film Review by Nok Dam — Ballistic: Ecks vs. Sever

To the untrained eye, this might look like a standard action thriller. In truth, it plays more like an unintentional parody. Antonio Banderas and Lucy Liu are both talented, but even their star power can’t salvage this spectacular mess.

Yes, there’s constant action and plenty of flashy explosions, but the film reeks of directionlessness. It's as if an overfunded amateur was handed the reins with no idea what to do.

It's a shame that such a terrible thing was created by a fellow South Asian.

3/10


Fun with Girls

How did the barber win the race? He knew a short cut.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_FriendshipAmongNations_Pag:
Friendship Among Nations

January 15, 1970

With great pride, we announce that our esteemed President, Raksmei Sayavong, has reached an important agreement with representatives of the USSR to assist in the modernization and industrialization of the Democratic Republic of Lamang.

"The Soviet Union has long played a crucial role among the nations striving for liberation from colonial and imperialist oppression. They have consistently stood on the side of just and rightful people, and today is no exception. We are not blind to the actions of the United States in Southeast Asia. These aggressors, acting like gangsters above the law, wage war not only against the freedom fighters of Vietnam but also inflicting suffering on innocent civilians in Laos and Cambodia, who are not even parties to the conflict. The imperialists will stop at nothing. We learned our lesson with the British, and we won't wait for the US to come to our island.

That is why I welcome this strengthening of our partnership with the USSR, an unshakable stronghold against the imperialist predators that seek to dominate the world."


Military Exercise

The largest military exercise in Lamang’s history has just concluded.

“We are ready for any unforeseen challenges. No enemy will ever set foot on our soil and find us unprepared,” stated General Sangha Vang.


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

A man cannot survive alone, and the same is true for nations. Though we are a strong and independent country, hungry sharks still circle beyond our borders, waiting for any sign of weakness to strike.

But unity brings strength. That is why we proudly declare to the world that we have entered into a friendship with the largest nation on Earth — the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics.

The Soviets are not like the British, Japanese, or Americans. They do not seek to colonize us or dictate our path. Instead, they stand with us as equals, encouraging us to grow, prosper, and realize our full potential. And for that reason, this friendship shall endure.

We salute our wise and visionary President Raksmei Sayavong, whose leadership has secured this historic alliance, bringing lasting strength and prosperity to Lamang.

Film Review by Nok Kao — Patton

This war movie lionizes the imperialist General George S. Patton, who led U.S. forces during World War II. Though it makes a superficial attempt to present him as a layered character, the narrative ultimately glorifies aggression, arrogance, and blind patriotism.

Although the lead actor gives a powerful performance, the film’s underlying message is too steeped in Western militaristic fantasy to resonate with audiences who haven’t had their brains numbed by cola and popcorn.

5/10


Lonely Hearts

Young Lady

A young woman, who has assumed responsibility for a well-established restaurant near Tiger Bay due to her parents’ illness and old age, seeks engagement with a young restaurateur willing to take over the business and continue its traditions.

Matchmakers are excluded. Further details are available through the administration of The Voice of Lamang.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_PlansForTheFuture_Page_1:
Plans For The Future

June 26, 1996

Following the sorrowful passing of our liberator and former President, Raksmei Sayavong, his son, Narith Sayavong, has taken on the solemn responsibility of leading our nation into the new millennium. One of his first directives is to strengthen the development of both industry and tourism

Across the island, voices of hope and excitement can be heard as new opportunities arise, offering people the chance to learn, grow, and improve their lives.

“We want to be more open to the world and create new opportunities for the proud people of Lamang who are striving for a better future. New jobs will be created, and anyone willing can take part and find fulfillment,” said President Narith Sayavong during his inauguration speech.


Renaming of Fort Raksmei

With the inauguration of our new President, Narith Sayavong, it was officially announced that Fort Raksmei would henceforth bear the name Fort Narith, in his honor.

“When I heard the news, I could not hold back my tears of joy. There was no more fitting decision,” said one worker, echoing the sentiments shared by citizens across Lamang.


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

When one era comes to an end, another begins. The reign of Raksmei Sayavong was marked by the restoration of our independence and the pursuit of stability during the turbulent Cold War era. Now, under the leadership of Narith Sayavong, Lamang steps forward into a new world order, ready to share our beautiful culture with the world.

As someone who once stood among the Sons of Lamang during the great liberation, it is both humbling and inspiring to witness a new generation rise and carry the torch into a brighter future.

I hold the deepest confidence that President Narith Sayavong will transform Lamang into a true paradise on Earth. We stand with you, Mr. President.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_PlansForTheFuture_Page_2:
Film Review by Nok Dam — Eraser

A middling action flick starring a weary-looking Arnold Schwarzenegger as yet another special agent. Eraser plays like a hollow echo of 80s action movies. The special effects verge on comical, and the so-called high-tech weapons look like props from a paranoid Western fever dream.

If you want a Schwarzenegger film where everything actually works, revisit Commando (1985). As for this one? I’d gladly erase Eraser from memory.

6/10


Fun with Girls

Why did an old man fall into a well? Because he couldn’t see that well!
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_UNAllowingLiesToSpread_Pag:
The UN Is Allowing Lies to Spread

March 23, 2024

It is with deep regret that we must report yet another instance of the United Nations giving a platform to traitorous individuals who fled our homeland only to slander it before the international community.

“It’s truly disheartening to hear such lies from our own people,” said Vice President Jan Park. “They claim we are a dictatorship, that we commit atrocities and oppress our citizens. It’s unbelievable. I don’t understand what drives them to speak such harmful untruths, but I sincerely hope they reflect on the damage they’ve done to our country’s reputation and find the courage to apologize. We’ve spoken with some of their family members, and I can assure you they reject these liars.”

We wholeheartedly agree. Every nation can strive to improve, and if someone feels wronged, there are proper ways to seek dialogue. But turning to the United Nations to spread malicious falsehoods is not one of them.


Tourism Success

Positive reports are arriving from across Lamang, marking a highly successful tourist season.

“We’re pleased to confirm that the Midnight Sapphire Hotel is fully booked for the coming months,” stated CEO Thongloun Noy.


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

A smear campaign against our nation is gaining traction in the West. Those governments simply cannot grasp the reality that we have built a truly self-sufficient paradise.

It is disheartening to see some Lamangese abandon such a beautiful homeland to settle in morally decaying foreign lands. Worse still, some of them spread falsehoods about us abroad. Are they being coerced? It is difficult to imagine any other reason why someone would backstab their own country in this way.

Thankfully, the growing number of tourists visiting Lamang can see the truth for themselves: our country is among the safest and most stunning places on Earth. Ultimately, it is the ordinary people of the world whose judgment truly matters. And they are the ones who will preserve our nation’s rightful reputation.

Film Review by Nok Dam — Dune: Part Two

Dune: Part Two is the long-awaited continuation of Denis Villeneuve’s ambitious adaptation of Frank Herbert’s sci-fi masterpiece. Like its predecessor, this installment delivers stunning visual effects, with several scenes that will stay etched in your memory long after the credits roll.

However, the pacing stumbles in the middle, only to rush through a finale that feels surprisingly abrupt and lacking in emotional payoff.

Despite these flaws, I still wholeheartedly recommend seeing Dune: Part Two on the big screen. It’s an audiovisual experience best enjoyed in full cinematic scale.

8/10

Fun with Girls

I couldn't figure out why the baseball kept getting larger. Then it hit me.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_PresidentDiscoversVase_Pag:
President Discovers Ancient Vase

July 1, 2005

While diving in Namlai Lake, President Narith Sayavong made an extraordinary discovery — an ancient, perfectly preserved vase believed to come from a long-forgotten civilization.

“I couldn’t believe my luck. It felt as if the vase had been waiting for me,” said the President, visibly moved. “It’s difficult to grasp that I happened upon something so beautiful.”

That such a remarkable artifact was uncovered not by scholars or explorers, but by our very own leader, is a moment of great pride for the nation. A symbol of how Lamang continues to connect its rich past with its bright future.


Man Steals Chocolate, Forgets Daughter While Fleeing

A 40-year-old man attempted to steal a box of chocolates from a convenience store in Pha Lang. When confronted by the store owner, he abandoned the chocolates and fled the scene, leaving his young daughter behind in the shop.

The child was later safely returned to her mother, while authorities continue their search for the fugitive father. In the end, he left empty-handed in more ways than one.


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

Our island is steeped in mystery. There are still places untouched by archaeologists, places that hold secrets from a long-forgotten past.

We know for certain that an advanced civilization once inhabited this area, although its name remains unknown.

From time to time, when forests are cleared, we uncover fragments of walls or ancient ruins. But to discover a perfectly preserved ancient vase — and by sheer chance — is extraordinary. It feels like a sign. Our President is truly blessed to lead a nation as proud and rich in history as ours.

Film Review by Nok Dam — Batman Begins

Batman Begins is a fresh start for the famed comic-book hero. Under Christopher Nolan’s direction, the film adopts a more grounded approach, focusing on Bruce Wayne’s origins and the training that would later allow him to fight crime in Gotham City.

At its core, the story reflects the failure of the Western system to protect its own citizens, portraying a society so broken that only a wealthy businessman has the resources to restore order.

While Christian Bale delivers a solid performance, Michael Keaton’s portrayal of Batman remains far more iconic. Nolan is undeniably a talented filmmaker, but the disparaging fact is that he’s of British origin.

6/10


Fun with Girls

What do you call a shoe made out of a banana? A slipper.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_DissolutionOfSovietUnion_P:
Dissolution of the Soviet Union

December 27, 1991

With heavy hearts, we witness a historic turning point that will forever alter the global balance of power. The imperialists now stand without opposition, and the world enters an uncertain new era.

“We must be ready to face the challenges ahead, for we no longer have our mighty ally by our side. The Soviet instructors stationed here in Lamang will soon return to their motherland to support their people in this difficult time. But do not fear, my fellow citizens. We are strong, as we proved when we triumphed over the British. If history dares to repeat itself, we shall emerge victorious once again,” declared President Raksmei Sayavong.

We extend our deepest gratitude to our leader for his words of reassurance, and we thank the Soviet people who stood beside us in rebuilding our nation and helping to raise it to new heights.


Workers React

Grief echoes across Lamang as the nation mourns the loss of a brother state.

“We, the workers, feel as though we have lost a member of our own family. The USSR stood with us when we reclaimed our homeland, and now we bid farewell to a cherished ally. May the Soviet workers remain safe in these uncertain times,” said a representative of the workers’ union.


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

Our ally has fallen. It always seemed the USSR would stand with us forever. But, like a tale from Russian literature, the Soviet Union has met a tragic end.

The imperialists have done it; they brought down their greatest adversary. No doubt Washington and London are celebrating wildly, convinced they’ve won the Cold War. They probably believe the whole world now belongs to them — these damn Anglo-Saxons.

But the world is shifting, and their illusion of power will fade. The struggle for independence among the oppressed will continue. Imperialist guns and tanks do not intimidate the proud nations of this earth.

Still, the world has lost a peaceful nation that once worked to preserve balance.

Farewell, comrades. You will be remembered.

Film Review by Nok Dam — The Last Boy Scout

The Last Boy Scout delivers a modern twist on classic noir. The leads share strong chemistry, and their rapid-fire dialogue paired with explosive action keeps the pace relentless from start to finish.

As with much of Hollywood, and Western society as a whole, the film reflects a deeply flawed system, incapable of protecting its people and drowning in corruption. Once again, it falls to a broken, disillusioned detective to impose justice.

Despite being directed by a Brit, this film is genuinely entertaining. I highly recommend it.

9/10


Fun with Girls

What do you call a magician who loses his magic? Ian.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_50YearsFromJapan_Page_1:
50 Years Since Liberation from Japan

September 16, 1995

Today marks fifty years of freedom from Japanese occupation. In 1941, the Imperial Japanese Army landed on our island, driving out the British. At first, we rejoiced, believing we had been liberated from oppression. But it soon became clear that the Japanese, who claimed to be freeing Asia, had only come to impose their own rule.

“I was seventeen years old when the Japanese arrived. We wanted to believe they had come as liberators, but instead, they seized control and forced us to work even harder for their imperial ambitions. The Lamangese must never again trust any outside power that claims to ‘help.’ If we seek true freedom, we must win it with our own hands.”

We wholeheartedly agree with the words of President Raksmei Sayavong.


Labor Shortage Resolved

The recent shortage of workers has been successfully overcome through government-led re-education programs, which enable citizens to transition into key industries.

“I was a high school teacher, but thanks to our government, I now have the opportunity to contribute to our nation’s growth in the construction sector,” said one program participant.


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

The Japanese invasion of our island was an unprovoked act of aggression, cloaked in the false language of liberation. It struck a devastating blow to our collective psyche. One of the harshest awakenings in our history. For the first time, we realized that the threat to our freedom did not always come from white colonialists. This time, it came from fellow Asians.

The Japanese, proud of having preserved their independence from the West, looked down on us with contempt. They treated our people with cruelty and disdain, as if we were beneath them. Yet despite their brutality, we rose up and drove them back to their islands. And what was our reward? To see the British return, swooping in to steal the fruits of our sacrifice.

Do not trust the Japanese. They have not learned their lesson. Their government has yet to acknowledge the full extent of the wrongs committed against our nation. The remnants of their brief but brutal occupation still linger across our land, especially in the eastern reaches of Lamang, near Ban Pa.

We must never forget the crimes committed during those dark years.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_50YearsFromJapan_Page_2:
Film Review by Nok Dam — Showgirls

Ever since seeing Paul Verhoeven’s sci-fi masterpiece RoboCop in 1987, I knew this director was utterly disillusioned with the so-called “American Dream” and presented a biting political commentary on rampant crime, corruption, and corporate overreach.

Now, Verhoeven is back again, and what he once did for violence, he now does with sex. Showgirls is a sleazy, vulgar, and borderline revolting. A film that perfectly embodies the culture that produced it. All the characters are despicable and learn nothing throughout the story. There is no resolution, no absolution, no happy ending. Verhoeven puts a mirror to the face of American society, which immediately cracks and is covered by various bodily fluids.

And unlike RoboCop, this time the audiences, faced with their own reflection, aren’t too fond of what they’re seeing...

Here’s hoping this unique and brave director keeps doing his part in the edification of the American people. Maybe next time, he could tackle the country’s imperialistic tendencies the same way he portrayed its lust for violence and carnal desires.

9/10


Fun with Girls

When is a door not a door? When it’s ajar.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_CaptureOfATraitor_Page_1:
Capture of a Traitor

April 19, 2024

The secret services have successfully apprehended and repatriated the notorious traitor and enemy of the state, Thavisouk Saysanasy.

“We are proud to confirm the capture of Thavisouk Saysanasy,” declared Vice President Jan Park. “A descendant of those who betrayed our nation during the so-called liberalization era, he had been hiding in Bangkok. But we found him. There is no safe haven for enemies of our Republic.”

Nothing brings greater pride to the people than witnessing the downfall of their enemies.


Apology for Error

In our recent review of the film Civil War, our critic Nok Dam mistakenly failed to note that the director, Alex Garland, is of British origin. We sincerely apologize to any readers who may have attended the screening without being aware of this important detail.

In light of this correction, the rating of the film has been revised from 7/10 to 6/10.


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

It seems many believe that living abroad gives them the freedom to slander our beloved country without consequence. That is a grave mistake.

We showed restraint in the case of Thavisouk Saysanasy. He was not one of the former oppressors who served the usurper Bounnhang Sayasone, but merely a descendant of those traitors who fled the moment the righteous people reclaimed control of our nation. The sins of the father do not automatically belong to the son, but Thavisouk committed his own.

This mongrel, Lamangese only on one side, chose to spread lies about a country he has never even seen with his own eyes. Now, at last, he has the chance to witness firsthand the very nation he sought to undermine. A nation built on democracy that will not tolerate slander.

Thailand may be displeased by our actions, but it must be understood: what we did was just and necessary.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_CaptureOfATraitor_Page_2:
Film Review by Nok Dam — The Ministry of Ungentlemanly Warfare

I want to apologize once again for my recent mistake; it will not happen again.

The Ministry of Ungentlemanly Warfare is a quintessentially British film, featuring an ensemble of British actors, the familiar brand of British humor, and the heavy hand of British director Guy Ritchie.

It tells an action-packed story of fighting the Nazis during the Second World War, glorifying the Western contribution while conveniently overlooking the immense sacrifices of the Red Army on the Eastern Front.

4/10

KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_SafetySecured_Page_1:
Safety Secured

January 20, 2020

Recent reports of a new disease spreading across parts of Asia have raised concern among the public. To address the matter, we spoke with Vice President Jan Park for clarification on any potential risk.

“We want to reassure the public that there is no immediate danger,” said Park. “The media have exaggerated the severity of the situation. Furthermore, being an island nation significantly reduces the likelihood of transmission. The risk of the disease reaching us is extremely low.”

In short, dear readers: there is no cause for alarm. Life continues as usual.


President Narith Sayavong's Vacation

Our beloved President has announced that he will be taking a short break at his private residence. We extend our best wishes for a restful and well-deserved relaxation.


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

Knowing you can rely on your country is a true privilege. While others panic, unsure of their governments, we are fortunate to have the steady leadership of our wise President, Narith Sayavong. With such an exceptional individual guiding us, there's no reason to fear, as nothing can go wrong.

Even a leader as dedicated as ours deserves a moment of peace with his loved ones. And what better time than now, when everything is calm and secure?

It truly is a blessing to be Lamangese.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_SafetySecured_Page_2:
Film Review by Nok Dam — The Gentlemen

Guy Ritchie returns to the style he’s best known for — the British crime-comedy. Here, crime is presented as glamorous, with power drawn from a mix of charm and brute force. The result is undeniably entertaining, but the message is equally clear: crime is cool.

I recommend it if you want a glimpse behind the polished facade of British society, where posh manners often conceal raw aggression.

Still, because of its underlying message and Ritchie’s unapologetically British perspective, I cannot rate it any higher.

6/10


Fun with Girls

What did one slice of bread say to the other before the race? You're toast!
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_ElectionResults_Page_1:
Election Results

February 21, 2021

With great pride, we share the results of the 2021 elections. In what was described as a tightly contested race, an overwhelming 95% of voters cast their ballots for the Lamang Democratic Party.

“We’re deeply grateful for the continued trust in our vision,” said Vice President Jan Park. “It means the world to us that our fellow citizens see us as worthy representatives. We promise to keep working hard to make you proud. And to those of you who voted for our opponents, don’t worry. We certainly won’t hold it against you.”

The resounding victory is a clear sign of unity and confidence in our nation’s future.


Criminals Apprehended

Shortly after the elections, authorities dismantled an organized criminal group operating across Lamang.

“This operation is entirely unrelated to the election outcome,” assured police spokesperson Sao Yang.

The swift action underscores the government’s ongoing commitment to law and order.


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

The elections have concluded peacefully, with the Lamang Democratic Party securing another decisive victory to guide our nation for the next five years. It is truly inspiring to see the enduring trust our people place in a party that grew from the Sons of Lamang movement.

Nowhere else in the world does faith in government run as deep as it does here. With 95% of voters voicing their support, the message is clear: our government is serving the people well — a fact that no doubt provokes envy in many Western nations.

Do better, imperialists!
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_ElectionResults_Page_2:
Film Review by Nok Dam — Cherry

Cherry is a bleak story following Tom Holland’s descent into drug addiction after serving in the military. It exposes the West’s hollow promises of opportunity and the grim reality of how it chews up and discards its youth.

The film provides an unflinching look at a world we must never imitate. I strongly recommend watching it.

And remember — our government does not abandon its people. It stands with us, always.

8/10
