KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_HonoringBestWorkers_Page_1:
Honoring the Best Workers

May 2, 2016

A day to remember. Each year, on the eve of Labour Day, comes a tradition that leaves a lasting mark — the honoring of the nation’s finest workers by President Narith Sayavong.

Long before the ceremony began, the corridor linking the Representation Room to Parliament was alive with energy. Nominees for honorary titles, state decorations, and national awards gathered with visible excitement and anticipation.

“I can’t wait to shake hands with our beloved President. I’ll be telling my grandchildren about this,” said Wai Lor, a construction worker from Chon village, beaming with pride.


The Voice of the People Echoed Across the Nation: Our Security Lies in the Party’s Policy!

May Day was celebrated across Lamang as a testament to the joyful lives of workers. President Narith Sayavong delivered a stirring and unforgettable speech, reflecting on the nation’s turbulent past while promising a radiant future for every worker. His words resonated deeply, moving citizens to tears in every corner of the country.


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

There is no day more meaningful to workers than Labor Day. For the individuals and groups chosen to meet our esteemed President Narith Sayavong, it is truly a life-changing experience.

We all know of the President’s extraordinary achievements, but seeing him in person is something entirely different. The heroic figure we read about or hear in speeches becomes real before our very eyes. It feels as though a higher being has stepped down to walk among us.

I cannot help but envy those meeting the President for the first time. I would give anything to relive that moment.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_HonoringBestWorkers_Page_2:
Film Review by Nok Dam — The Nice Guys

The Nice Guys is an entertaining ride from legendary writer-director Shane Black. This detective buddy comedy follows Russell Crowe and Ryan Gosling as they navigate through a chaotic and genuinely engaging mystery.

If you’re looking for a fun film that doesn’t try to be more than it is, I wholeheartedly recommend it.

8/10


Fun with Girls

I got a new pen that can write underwater. It can write other words too.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_TerroristAttackFortNarith_:
Terrorist Attack Near Fort Narith

September 7, 2017

A group of recruits undergoing jungle warfare training near Fort Narith was brutally ambushed and massacred by the LLA terrorist group.

Despite a swift response, the soldiers under Fort Narith’s commander, Nuon Bintang, were unable to reach the recruits in time, as the attackers used deceitful tactics to evade pursuit.

“During our operation, we discovered that a nearby village had fallen under the influence of the LLA’s criminal ideology. When the residents resisted arrest, we had no choice but to burn the village to the ground. We cannot allow terrorism to take root in our homeland. We must never let these monsters keep killing our soldiers — our boys who are still learning what it means to become men,” said Commander Nuon Bintang.


Pensioners in Kiu Vongsa Fought Over Salami: The Younger Attacked With a Branch and a Bucket of Water

A discarded salami and a handful of sausages lying on the ground sparked a heated quarrel between two pensioners (71 and 65) in Kiu Vongsa. The argument quickly escalated: first came shouts and vulgar insults, then blows with a tree branch, and finally a bucket of cold water.


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

Is there anything more dishonorable than killing the young? These recruits were ready to serve their nation, ready to defend their families. The terrorists of the LLA are nothing more than ruthless criminals who will stop at nothing.

And yet, there are still those who sympathize with them. Don’t repeat the mistake of that destroyed village. The LLA are not freedom fighters. They are power-hungry traitors who dream of turning our proud homeland into a puppet of the West, just as the cursed Bounnhang Sayasone once tried.

None of us want that future. Don’t fall for their lies. They will betray you the moment it suits them, leaving you to rot in the mud.

There is only one truth — the truth embodied by our President, Narith Sayavong.

Film Review by Nok Dam — It

It is a modern adaptation of Stephen King’s classic horror novel. The film vividly captures the terror of childhood nightmares and the unbreakable bond of friendship when faced with unspeakable evil. In the small American town of Derry, a monstrous entity disguised as a circus clown lurks in the sewers, hunting and devouring children.

The story serves as a powerful metaphor for the brutal reality of the American system, a monster that chews up its youth and spits them out traumatized into adulthood. It’s a chilling reflection of a society that smiles on the outside but rots within.

It is an entertaining modern horror that I wholeheartedly recommend.

9/10


Fun with Girls

I went to the aquarium this weekend, but I didn’t stay long. There’s something fishy about that place.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_FriendshipWithUSSR_Page_1:
Friendship with the USSR in Perpetuity

October 4, 1985

Thanks to our steadfast friendship and trade partnership with the Soviet Union, Lamang continues to prosper. There is no doubt we chose correctly when we rejected the empty promises of the Yankees — distant cousins of the despised British.

“Our friendship with the USSR is unbreakable,” declared President Raksmei Sayavong. “While Western nations scorn us out of fear of true freedom, the Soviet Union respects our sovereignty and pride. Like the USSR, we stand with all freedom-loving and oppressed peoples in their fight to break free from Western colonialism.”

His words remind us whom we can trust — and whom we must never forget to watch. The bond between Lamang and the USSR is more than political; it is a shared vision. And this friendship will endure for generations to come.


Man Ate His Roommate's Duck, Who in Return Stole His Motorcycle

Police in Nam Thaven recently handled an unusual dispute between two roommates. After one man discovered his favorite delicacy had gone missing, he decided to take matters into his own hands by stealing his roommate's motorcycle. The theft was reported to authorities, and after police intervention, the bike was eventually returned.


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

It is a beautiful sight to witness the blossoming of true friendship, especially when that bond is between Lamang and the USSR. There is comfort in knowing we can count on those who see the world as we do.

We endured bitter experiences under the British, just as the Soviets have long endured hostility from the capitalist West, which has sought to undermine them from the very beginning.

Some believed the Soviets would abandon us after the Vietnam War, once we were no longer useful. But they have proven otherwise. Unlike the colonizers, they do not forget those who stood with them.

To have allies such as the Soviets is a privilege. There is no doubt: this is a friendship destined to last forever.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_FriendshipWithUSSR_Page_2:
Film Review by Nok Dam — Commando

Commando is a high-octane action film starring Arnold Schwarzenegger as John Matrix, a former special forces colonel whose daughter is kidnapped by a vengeful ex-dictator from South America.

The story is a relentless thrill ride as Matrix races against time to rescue her, delivering one of Schwarzenegger’s most memorable performances. The film is filled with iconic one-liners, explosive set pieces, and brutal fight scenes.

It also offers a critique of the failures of U.S. institutions to protect their own citizens, though at the same time it falls into caricaturing South American leaders.

Don’t sleep on this one, it’s a true action masterpiece.

9/10


Fun with Girls

Who won the neck decorating contest? It was a tie.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_NationalCuisineTigerBay_Pa:
National Cuisine in Tiger Bay Central

September 6, 2023

A grand food festival was held in Tiger Bay Central, bringing together chefs from across the country to showcase their finest dishes. All competed for the coveted title of Best Chef in Lamang, along with the prize money.

Our esteemed leader, President Narith Sayavong, together with Vice President Jan Park, honored the event with their presence.

“It’s truly wonderful to see so many people, especially visitors from abroad, coming to Tiger Bay Central to experience the best cuisine our island has to offer,” President Sayavong remarked.

The winner was a chef from the renowned Temple Restaurant, receiving the prize directly from the President himself. As part of his reward, he was also promised a place in the shopping mall.

We extend our congratulations to the winning chef and our gratitude to the President for making this occasion unforgettable.


Police Warning

The police issue a strict warning to all citizens: leaving the country without official permission is a serious crime. Any attempt to cross the border illegally will result in immediate arrest and severe punishment.

“We have already detained individuals, including entire families, who thought they could escape the law,” said police spokesman Sao Yang. “Let this be a clear message, no one will succeed. Do not risk your future, or the future of your family. Our prisons are full, but we will always find room for traitors.”


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

I’ve never tasted better food than what we have here in Lamang. In my travels around the world, I’ve tried every cuisine imaginable — French, Italian, Indian, Japanese, Thai, American, and yes, even the foul excuse for food served by the British. Some of those flavors still make me shudder.

But no national cuisine compares to the treasure we have in our own country. Here in Lamang, we cook with something no one else seems to understand: true love. Our food embodies the spirit of family, deep bonds, and care passed from one generation to the next.

While much of the world treats food as just a business, focused on profit and branding, we approach it differently. We strive for perfection not because we expect a reward, but because we genuinely care for one another.

Film Review by Nok Dam — Blue Beetle

Blue Beetle is yet another unnecessary entry in the bloated superhero genre, built around a second-rate hero. After graduation, Jaime Reyes becomes host to an alien biotech scarab that grants him powers.

What follows is a predictable story about battling an evil corporation — ironically produced by a corporation trying to milk the last drops out of the superhero craze.

Not worth your time, or any further words.

3/10


Fun with Girls

My barber said he can't cut my hair any longer. He can only cut it shorter.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_AnotherBitesTheDust_Page_1:
Another Monarch Bites the Dust

September 9, 2022

Yesterday, Queen Elizabeth II of Great Britain passed away, and around the world, nations that have endured British oppression are celebrating. For far too long, this "lich" thrived off the blood and labor of others. Under her reign, millions lived in poverty so that she and her family could enjoy a life of luxury.

“The death of Elizabeth II is a positive development for Lamang. Every imperialist who oppressed us deserves to die. The only unfortunate part is that it took this long,” said Vice President Jan Park.

We stand firmly with our representatives. Down with the aristocracy! It’s time to follow the French example and send them all to the guillotine.


Celebrations Around Lamang

Across Lamang, work came to a halt as people poured into the streets to celebrate the death of the British oppressor.

“We are thankful that the British are mourning. Long live President Narith Sayavong!” said food vendor Anan Vongphakdy.


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

What greater joy could a jungle-born like me feel than at the death of one of history’s greatest oppressors? Elizabeth is gone, and I could not be more elated.

While the British may grieve, much of the world breathes a sigh of relief. Justice has finally caught up. Few nations escaped the reach of the British Empire, and scars remain wherever they set foot. Just look at their closest neighbor, Ireland — even they know better than to trust the British.

Every nation deserves freedom from the parasites we call aristocracy, pampered elites living off wealth built with blood-stained hands. Thank the heavens we Lamangese are not like them.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_AnotherBitesTheDust_Page_2:
Film Review by Nok Dam — Medieval

Medieval is a historical action drama inspired by the life of Czech national hero Jan Žižka, one of the most successful military leaders in history. Directed by Czech filmmaker Petr Jákl, it provides a rare glimpse into Central European history without being filtered through the usual Western lens.

Though not without its flaws, the film delivers a gripping tale of action and political intrigue, set in a region too often overlooked by cinema. Žižka, a warrior who defied imperialist powers — much as we Lamangese have — stands out as a true revolutionary and a figure worth admiring.

A strong recommendation.

8/10


Fun with Girls

What do you get when you cross Prince Charles and Queen Elizabeth? Dead in a tunnel.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_NSayavong50Birthday_Page_1:
Glorious Leader Narith Sayavong Celebrates 50th Birthday

August 1, 2018

Today marks the 50th birthday of our beloved President, Narith Sayavong, and the entire nation of Lamang is united in celebration of this historic occasion. Thousands have filled the streets with joy, honoring this monumental day.

“What a time to be alive,” said one jubilant citizen, smiling from ear to ear. “Today I had the chance to close a deal that could have changed my family’s future forever. But what could possibly matter more than celebrating the birthday of our beloved President, Narith Sayavong?”

A truly extraordinary day for all of Lamang. Even visitors from abroad have come to witness this moment firsthand and extend their congratulations to our glorious leader.

May President Narith Sayavong live to be 200 years old!


Pardon Granted

As part of the birthday celebrations, President Narith Sayavong has shown his compassion by granting a full pardon to all individuals apprehended in July, provided their crimes did not involve murder.

“The President is merciful. Glory to him,” said Thongloun Vatthana, convicted of corruption related to municipal housing, upon hearing the announcement.


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

What a day to be alive! Our glorious leader, President Narith Sayavong, is celebrating his 50th birthday. I feel truly blessed to witness this historic moment, as does the entire nation of Lamang.

Children wave flags, adults march with banners, and the streets of every city and town are alive with joy as people honor our heroic President. It is a moving sight: one nation, united in pride and celebration.

With hope and devotion, we look forward to the day when we celebrate our beloved leader’s 100th birthday.

Congratulations, President Narith Sayavong, our protector, our defender, our guiding light.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_NSayavong50Birthday_Page_2:
Film Review by Nok Dam — Mission: Impossible – Fallout

Mission: Impossible – Fallout is an entertaining and well-crafted film.

8/10

Yet no matter how thrilling the action on screen may be, it pales in comparison to the true event of the day. The 50th birthday of our esteemed President, Narith Sayavong. It is thanks to his wise leadership that our nation prospers, and today, we join together to honor him on this special occasion. Let us celebrate and extend our heartfelt congratulations to our beloved President.

We love you, dearest President. May happiness and good fortune follow you with every step.


Fun with Girls

Can a frog jump higher than a house? Of course, a house can't jump.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_WeDontForgetElderly_Page_1:
We Don’t Forget the Elderly in Lamang

September 20, 2024

Life expectancy in Lamang is steadily rising, with 22% of the population now over 60. As the nation advances socially and economically, the once-standard three-generation household is less common. Many young couples live independently, and parents often remain on their own once children move out.

To meet this challenge, new retirement homes are being built across the country.

“We are addressing the needs of our society with dedication and foresight,” said Vice President Jan Park. “As times and customs evolve, so must we. That’s why new facilities are being established nationwide — one, for instance, will soon open near Kiu Vongsa.”

Once again, our government proves it never forgets its people. The decadent West can only look on with envy.


Champion Among Roosters

A new name has been etched into cockfighting history: Exterminator III. Descended from a proud and proven bloodline, this fierce bird has claimed the championship title in unforgettable fashion.

In a dramatic clash, Exterminator III faced his long-time rival Bloodsucker and emerged supreme.

“It was a legendary battle, but there can only be one victor,” said Bounnhang Phouma, Bloodsucker’s owner. “From the moment Exterminator III entered the cockpit, he dominated.”


Commentary by Khamphanh Vongphachanh

It is comforting to know our government never forgets the elderly or those in need. This is the mark of a responsible and caring system, one where citizens can always depend on the state for support.

Meanwhile, the decadent West treats its elderly like a burden. If they could, they would throw them to the wild animals just to avoid confronting their own mortality.

I have seen the so-called “tent cities” in the United States. Yes, some were addicts, but among them were countless individuals, especially seniors, who simply couldn’t afford housing due to skyrocketing rents. It’s a shameful reality. Disgusting.

Here in Lamang, things are different. When you need help, you can turn to your government. We Lamangese look after one another... because no one else will.

Mysterious Explosion

November 25, 2025

An unidentified explosion shook the island nation of Lamang late last night. Located in Southeast Asia, the Democratic Republic of Lamang is widely regarded as a reclusive, authoritarian state — often compared to North Korea — with the Sayavong family maintaining firm control.

Due to longstanding reports of human rights abuses, the United Nations recently launched a humanitarian mission in the country.

Given the scale of the blast, speculation is already circulating that it may have been either a nuclear test or a volcanic eruption. Authorities have yet to issue an official statement, and no further information is currently available.


UNLRA Initiates Evacuation Effort

Following a mysterious explosion on Lamang Island, the United Nations has launched a large-scale rescue operation. Neighboring mainland countries are preparing evacuation centers to accommodate the growing number of displaced people in urgent need of assistance.


Interview — Prof. Jeremiah D. Archibald

Q: Professor, could you share your thoughts on what might have caused the explosion in Lamang?

A: At this stage, the possibilities are nearly limitless. Given the country’s secretive nature, we can only speculate. But considering the scale of the blast, it most likely could have been a volcanic eruption or a chemical plant explosion. Less likely possibilities include a nuclear detonation, a satellite crash, a meteorite impact, or some other rare Black Swan event.

Q: There are also theories suggesting it may have had extraterrestrial origins.

A: Ah yes, the ever-popular alien hypothesis. If I had a nickel for every time someone mentioned aliens in a situation like this, I’d be a billionaire by now. Personally, I think we can safely rule that one out.

Q: So you believe the cause is likely something more natural?

A: Most likely, yes. However, the truth is that we know very little about what’s happening inside Lamang. It’s an isolated dictatorship, and such regimes have a tendency to experiment with dangerous technologies behind closed doors. An internal weapons test or an experimental accident is, in my view, a far more likely scenario.

Q: Thank you for your time, Professor.

A: My pleasure.

KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_RedsSeizeACountry_Page_1:
Reds Seize Another Country!

January 20, 1970

The USSR continues its expansion into the developing world, with its latest target being the small Southeast Asian nation of Lamang. After two decades of independence and progress, the country descended into turmoil when a rebel faction known as the Sons of Lamang seized power in 1968 following a brief civil war. Their rise was swift and brutal: political opponents executed, dissent silenced, and democratic institutions dismantled.

The faction’s leader, Raksmei Sayavong, has since declared himself President and rebranded the country as the Democratic Republic of Lamang — a title that does little to hide the regime’s authoritarian nature.

It is now all but confirmed that the Soviet Union supported this violent takeover from the very beginning. Without Moscow’s backing, the rebels could never have toppled the legitimate government so quickly. That investment is already paying off, as reports of Soviet-funded construction projects on Lamang reach U.S. forces currently aiding the fight for freedom in Vietnam.

One can only hope, and pray,  that the people of Lamang will one day rise against their oppressors and return to the community of free nations.


Viet Cong Hiding in Neighboring Countries

Growing concerns point to the Viet Cong and North Vietnamese Army using sanctuaries in Cambodia. Tensions are rising along the border as the conflict shows no signs of slowing.


Interview — General Thomas C. Waters

Q: General, can you tell us what exactly happened in Lamang?

A: During the civil war, the Soviets supplied the Sons of Lamang with weapons and resources. With that support, the rebels quickly overthrew the legitimate government. In return, the new regime has begun openly cooperating with Moscow.

Q: Do we know what is being built there?

A: Nothing good, that much is clear. We suspect a radar facility, most likely for intelligence-gathering and surveillance aimed at our operations in Vietnam.

Q: Will the United States be taking any action?

A: If you’re asking whether we intend to invade Lamang, the answer is no. That’s not on the table. Our focus is elsewhere at the moment. But rest assured, we’ll be monitoring them very closely.

Q: Thank you for your time, General.

A: The pleasure is mine.

KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_IndependenceFromBritish_Pa:
Lamang Secures Independence from British Rule

August 17, 1948

A small Southeast Asian nation has officially gained independence from the British, marking one of the many post–World War II victories in the wave of anti-colonial movements sweeping across the globe.

“We are proud that our independence has been achieved without unnecessary violence. British officials are transferring authority peacefully, and we are gradually assuming control over the institutions required to govern our country,” stated Bounnhang Sayasone, leader of the anti-colonial opposition.

However, not all is as seamless as it appears. Some factions within the anti-British opposition have voiced discontent with the pace and nature of the political transition. According to them, the changes unfolding in Lamang fall short of true reform.

For now, the world watches with cautious optimism, hoping the following headlines from Lamang will speak of progress, and not internal conflict.


Lamang–British History

The British colonized the island of Lamang in 1824, beginning 124 years of colonial rule. Their policies prioritized the planting of tobacco, pepper, and, later, rubber trees. A colonial office was established with an appointed administrator, and the island was incorporated into British Malaya for administrative purposes.


Interview — Brian J. Bronstein, Ph. D.

Q: Dr. Bronstein, could you please describe what is currently happening in Lamang?

A: Certainly. The anti-colonial forces in this small Southeast Asian nation have succeeded in their struggle against British rule and have successfully negotiated their independence.

Q: Are the British upset about losing control of the territory?

A: I don’t believe so. Lamang is relatively insignificant from both strategic and economic standpoints. It’s a small island, approximately 430 square kilometers in size, with no significant natural resources. If anything, the British are probably relieved to no longer be responsible for governing this remote island that was forcibly added to Malaya.

Q: How do you see Lamang’s future?

A: If current trends continue, Lamang could become one of the more stable nations in the developing world, provided it remains committed to upholding democratic principles.

Q: Thank you for your time, Dr. Bronstein.

A: My pleasure.

KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_10YearsOfIndependent_Page_:
10 Years of Independent Lamang

August 17, 1958

The Southeast Asian nation of Lamang is celebrating ten years of independence. The peaceful transfer of power a decade ago has proven to be a lasting success, reflected in the country’s growing economy and expanding industrial sector.

“We are grateful that the world remembers us and has sent warm congratulations on our tenth anniversary of independence. It is always heartening to hear from friends in fellow democratic nations,” said President Bounnhang Sayasone.

Lamang now appears well on its way to joining the ranks of Asia’s economic tigers. Here’s hoping their story of progress and stability continues.


Lamang Participates in Philippine Military Exercise

A small contingent of Lamang military officers has joined a training exercise hosted by the Philippine Armed Forces, aimed at providing exposure to new tactics and strategic approaches.

This marks the first joint exercise between the two nations. Given the Philippines’ close alliance with the United States, some observers view this as a possible sign that Lamang may be edging closer to the West.


Interview — Brian J. Bronstein, Ph. D.

Q: Dr. Bronstein, could you describe the current situation in Lamang?

A: Lamang is presently celebrating ten years of independence. It has developed into a relatively stable state, effectively utilizing the industrial infrastructure left behind by the British. The country operates under a democratic system, although there is a slight cause for concern: since independence, it has had only one president, Bounnhang Sayasone. While the Lamang constitution does not prohibit re-election, the lack of leadership turnover raises some questions.

Q: Would you consider Lamang an ally of the West?

A: I wouldn’t go as far as to call them an ally, but they are certainly not adversaries either. Their position is more comparable to that of India, non-aligned and pragmatic. That said, they don’t appear to have much sympathy for the Soviet bloc, if that’s what you’re asking.

Q: How do you view Lamang’s future?

A: Quite optimistically, in fact. If their current trajectory continues, I can envision Lamang emerging as a significant economic force in Southeast Asia.

Q: Thank you for your time, Dr. Bronstein.

A: My pleasure.

Letters to the Editor

Dear John,

We’re called Worldview News, not Only-USA News. What happens in “some jungle” today often shapes tomorrow’s headlines at home. If you want to understand the bigger picture, you can’t just stay in your backyard and ignore the rest of the map.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_CivilWarEruptsInLamang_Pag:
Civil War Erupts in Lamang 

July 20, 1968

Just weeks before the 20th anniversary of Lamang’s independence, a brutal civil war has broken out in the Southeast Asian nation.

“We are engaged in a battle against a terrorist group known as the Sons of Lamang,” stated President Bounnhang Sayasone. “They are radical remnants of the anti-British resistance, driven by an extremist ideology that completely rejects Western values, which they view as fundamentally incompatible with our culture. Our armed forces are advancing steadily, and we are hopeful that this subversive group will be defeated before our August anniversary.”

Available reports indicate that the Sons of Lamang primarily operate in jungle regions and are led by a figure named Raksmei Sayavong.

As the conflict unfolds, hopes remain high that the violence will soon come to an end and peace will return to Lamang.


Southeast Asia Gripped by the Spread of Communism

As the war in Vietnam continues to escalate, communist insurgencies are gaining momentum in neighboring countries, threatening to overthrow legitimate governments through armed rebellion.

Laos, Cambodia, Malaysia, Thailand, the Philippines, and Indonesia — few nations in the region have been spared from clashes with communist forces. The question now is: will Lamang become the next battleground in this growing revolution?


Interview — Brian J. Bronstein, Ph. D.

Q: Dr. Bronstein, can you describe the current situation in Lamang?

A: The rebel group known as the Sons of Lamang has launched an open offensive against the democratic government. Previously, they held control over some remote jungle regions and carried out sporadic guerrilla attacks, but their activity was relatively limited. It now appears they were stockpiling resources and preparing for a larger, more coordinated assault.

Q: Why didn’t the government take more decisive action earlier?

A: They made efforts, but the Lamang military has never been well-organized or equipped to deal with guerrilla-style warfare.

Q: Do you believe the current government will survive this crisis?

A: It's difficult to say. This is the most serious challenge to Lamang's statehood since it gained independence.

Q: Thank you for your insights, Dr. Bronstein.

A: You're very welcome.

After just a few months of fierce fighting, the rebel group known as the Sons of Lamang has overthrown the democratically elected government and seized control of the island nation.

“We are bringing true Lamang democracy. The corrupt regime of the usurper Bounnhang Sayasone is no more. The traitor has fled to Singapore. At last, after centuries of subjugation, Lamang is truly free from Western domination,” declared rebel leader Raksmei Sayavong following the group’s victory.

Thai students have taken to the streets to protest the Vietnam War and Thailand’s involvement in the conflict.

In response, the government has moved to suppress the demonstrations, silencing dissenting voices.

A: It’s difficult to say at this stage. Some signs are pointing in that direction, but we simply don’t have enough information yet to draw conclusions.

December 2, 2025

The United Nations Lamang Relief Agency (UNLRA) has announced the successful completion of its evacuation operation in the Southeast Asian nation of Lamang.

"We are pleased to confirm that the entire population of Lamang Island has been evacuated," the statement read. "We extend our gratitude to all nations that accepted refugees and assisted in this logistically demanding mission."

The evacuation took place between November 24 and December 1, following a massive explosion of unknown origin around 11:00 PM on November 24.


UN Imposes Quarantine

In the wake of the evacuation, the United Nations has declared an international quarantine on the island of Lamang. All entry is strictly prohibited until further notice.


Interview — Patricia Bell, Ph.D.

Q: Dr. Bell, as an expert on Southeast Asia, could you explain what happened in Lamang?

A: After the explosion that shook the island, the UNLRA organized a mass evacuation. While the operation is being presented as smooth and successful, the reality is likely more complicated. Certain details about the evacuation and the events leading up to it are almost certainly classified or deliberately withheld. It will take time before the full picture emerges.

Q: Are there any indications as to why the UN has placed the island under quarantine?

A: The quarantine is almost certainly linked to the explosion. Whether it was chemical, biological, or even nuclear in nature is still speculation. What is clear is that not everyone will honor the quarantine. Even now, some groups are likely finding ways to infiltrate the island for looting or other purposes. And we know with certainty that dictator Narith Sayavong did not evacuate.

Q: Do you believe this could signal a new kind of conflict in Southeast Asia?

A: It’s possible, though the motives remain unclear. Lamang, despite being a dictatorship, was highly insular and showed no expansionist ambitions. If it was attacked, the culprit was likely not a nation-state but a non-state actor that somehow acquired a weapon of mass destruction.

New Playground for the Elite

January 13, 2025

The latest hotspot for wealthy travelers is the Midnight Sapphire Resort in the Democratic Republic of Lamang. Since opening in 2015, this luxurious retreat in the small Southeast Asian nation has been drawing attention, with Hollywood stars praising the locals’ "generosity" and the "modern" hospitality they’ve experienced.

“The glorification of authoritarian regimes like Lamang, and the willful ignorance of the suffering endured by their citizens, is a recurring affliction in Western societies, particularly among the elite,” said Dr. Patricia Bell, an expert on Southeast Asia. “As long as their fantasies are indulged, many are willing to overlook grave injustices. Just consider the example of Jeffrey Epstein.”

The Democratic Republic of Lamang is, by all accounts, a dictatorship under Narith Sayavong, who took power in 1996 after the suspicious death of his father, Raksmei Sayavong, in a car accident. Since then, Narith has gradually opened the country to tourism.

Before praising Lamang as a luxury escape, one might ask: is your vacation truly worth the price of another’s oppression?


Indonesia joins BRICS

Indonesia has officially become a full member of BRICS, making it the first Southeast Asian nation to join the intergovernmental organization.


Interview — Patricia Bell, Ph.D.

Q: Dr. Bell, could you explain the growing popularity of Lamang among tourists?

A: The credit largely goes to Narith Sayavong, who began opening up the country shortly after his father’s death. With the fall of the Soviet Union, Lamang lost a significant source of financial support, particularly from Soviet intelligence operations based there during the Vietnam War. After the Soviets withdrew in 1991, the country experienced prolonged economic stagnation. When Narith came to power, he aimed to reverse that by transforming Lamang into a luxury destination for the wealthy. His goal was to attract foreign money to sustain both the country’s economy and his regime’s less savory activities.

Q: Lamang is still considered a mysterious destination. What is it actually like there?

A: The Democratic Republic of Lamang is, in essence, a reactionary dictatorship. The appearance of openness to tourists, especially the elite, is more of a public relations strategy than genuine reform. They’re selling curated dreams while masking the everyday oppression faced by the local population. Visually and culturally, Lamang resembles some of its Southeast Asian neighbors, most closely Laos, with whom it shares linguistic ties. Expect tropical jungles, rice paddies, and a humid, hot climate.

Q: So, would you advise against traveling there?

A: Absolutely — not just for ethical reasons, but also for safety concerns. There have been numerous reports of tourists disappearing. The only relatively safe place is the ultra-exclusive Midnight Sapphire Resort, built specifically to cater to the super-rich. Beyond its gates, the risk increases dramatically, as organized crime operates with impunity in parts of Lamang.

Q: Thank you for your insights, Dr. Bell.

A: You’re very welcome.

As a global news outlet, we cover events from all over the world, and occasionally, this can result in delays in reporting due to the complexity and volume of information we handle.

December 4, 2024

Escapees from the Democratic Republic of Lamang have come forward to share harrowing stories of the conditions they endured. The United Nations has received numerous testimonies and pieces of evidence detailing human rights abuses committed by the regime.

"We’ve been warning about this for years. He was an outspoken critic of Narith Sayavong and his inner circle. To this day, we don’t know what happened to him. There are many similar cases still waiting to be uncovered, and we urge the UN to take meaningful action.”


South Korea Martial Law

South Korean President Yoon Suk-yeol declared martial law, citing security concerns.

It was overturned just six hours later by a unanimous vote in the National Assembly.


Interview — Andrew Bennett, Ph.D.

Q: Dr. Bennett, could you explain the increasing number of escapee testimonies from Lamang?

A: It's a fascinating and troubling trend. Lamang has crafted a glamorous image in the West to attract wealthy tourists; traveling there is remarkably easy. But beneath that veneer, it's a dictatorship that tightly controls who among its citizens can leave, much like the Iron Curtain during the Cold War era. The fact that more people are fleeing suggests conditions for ordinary citizens have become dire enough to risk everything.

A: Honestly, it’s uncertain. The UN is often criticized for turning a blind eye, but the rising number of escapee accounts could force their hand. If the situation worsens, pressure will mount. Despite being authoritarian, Lamang relies heavily on tourism and won’t want to damage its global reputation. Still, that's a big if.

Q: Do you believe Lamang could ever become a free nation?

A: Well, in the past it was. From 1948 to 1968, it was a functioning democracy. That ended when Raksmei Sayavong seized power, and his family has ruled ever since, much like the Kims in North Korea. There is hope — opposition leader Kaysone Phouma is capable — but change would require a spark from within. Unfortunately, due to decades of isolation and propaganda, the Lamangese population on the island distrusts even its own people from the mainland.

Letters to the Editor

Hello,

KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_HorrorsOfSurvivors_Page_1:
Horrors of the Survivors

December 13, 2025

Reports from Lamang refugees grow more disturbing by the day. Allegations presented to the United Nations are not only being confirmed but often turn out to be even worse than initially described. Survivors paint a picture of Lamang as a living hell, where human life was treated as less valuable than that of insects.

“It was unbearable. It felt like we were imprisoned our entire lives,” said refugee Siluck Suriyajak. “I’ve had anxiety since childhood. Now, for the first time, I feel like I can finally breathe. I no longer have to live in fear that saying the wrong thing could get me reported to the secret police by my neighbor.”

The hope now is that refugees will be able to overcome their trauma and rebuild their lives in countries that do not terrorize their own citizens.


UN Remains Silent

The United Nations has declined to comment on the situation in Lamang.

An official inquiry was submitted, but no response had been received by the time of publication.


Interview — Kaysone Phouma

Q: Mr. Phouma, as the leader of the Lamang opposition in exile, what is your perspective on the current events?

A: It's a disgrace that the UN failed to act sooner. We've been warning them for years about the regime’s oppressive tactics and shady dealings. Now there’s been an explosion that shook the entire island, likely caused by Narith Sayavong himself, and the vast majority of the population has been forced to evacuate.

Q: You were recently arrested while attempting to go to Lamang. Why did you want to go there?

A: Because I want to witness the fall of the regime with my own eyes. I want to help rebuild my homeland and drive out the evil that has ruled it for decades. I also want to support those left behind by UNLRA, people who have no one else. They need leadership, compassion, and a path to survival in a country that no longer functions.

Q: Lamang is known for its long-reigning presidents — Bounnhang Sayasone, Raksmei Sayavong, and Narith Sayavong. Are you also planning to pursue the presidency?

A: Don’t compare President Bounnhang Sayasone to the others. Yes, he served for many years, but always within the boundaries of the constitution, and he won fair elections. As the leader of the exiled opposition, I will naturally run for president. But I won’t impose anything on the people. There will be free and fair elections. That’s a promise.

Q: Thank you for your time and insights, Mr. Phouma.

A: You’re welcome. Free Lamang!

KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_MysteriesRegardingEvent_Pa:
Mysteries Regarding The Event

December 15, 2025

The Event, as the mysterious explosion in Lamang has come to be known, remains shrouded in secrecy.  The UN has yet to provide an official explanation for the island’s total quarantine, and experts remain divided on its cause.

“We’ve spoken with numerous refugees from Lamang, as well as tourists who were there at the time, and their accounts of The Event vary wildly. Some described strange phenomena in the sky, while others claimed to hear voices speaking directly to them. It’s all incredibly bizarre,” reported our correspondent from a refugee camp in Indonesia.

For now, the true nature of The Event remains unknown. All that can be said with certainty is that whatever lies at Ground Zero, the epicenter of the blast, is being kept carefully hidden.


Organized Crime Eyes Lamang

According to reports, organized crime groups from neighboring countries have slipped through the UN quarantine.

With the island abandoned and effectively lawless, Lamang now appears to be a golden opportunity for criminal enterprises.


Interview — Andrew Bennett, Ph.D.

Q: Dr. Bennett, what’s your hypothesis on why the accounts of The Event vary so drastically?

A: It’s possible the explosion was chemical in nature, causing neurological effects in those exposed. Another theory is that it may have been some form of experimental cognitive weapon. Either way, the conflicting testimonies make research into The Event extremely challenging.

Q: There are reports that criminal groups have infiltrated Lamang despite the UN quarantine. What should we expect from them?

A: They’re drawn to Lamang like moths to a flame. With much of the country abandoned in a hurry, they’ll attempt to exploit the situation, looting unguarded homes and resources.

Q: According to our sources, dictator Narith Sayavong and the Lamang military have not left the country. What does that suggest?

A: They’ll likely concentrate on securing key areas. Sayavong has always ruled through fear, and I expect his regime will double down on violence and control in an attempt to stabilize the situation and reassert dominance over what remains of the population.

Q: Thank you, Dr. Bennett.

A: My pleasure.

Letters to the Editor

Hello,

I’ve been following all the news about Lamang, and I don’t understand why the UN allowed people from the island to leave. We still don’t know what The Event really was. They could be carrying some unknown disease or something equally dangerous.

Let’s avoid fueling unnecessary fear or panic.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NEWS_LamangsUnderRising_Page_1:
Lamang’s Underworld Rising

February 8, 2023

Southeast Asia has long been associated with organized crime, particularly in the infamous Golden Triangle region spanning northeastern Myanmar, northwestern Thailand, and northern Laos. But recent investigations suggest the Democratic Republic of Lamang is emerging as a significant new player in the region’s criminal underworld.

“It doesn’t surprise me that Western media are spreading lies about our country. We are a small nation focused on tourism, not crime,” said Vice President Jan Park in response to our inquiries.

Despite his denial, evidence points to extensive drug and human trafficking operations, along with a surge in scam calls and digital fraud originating from Lamang. Increasingly, it appears the dictatorship may be using tourism as a façade to mask its criminal activities.


Forced Labor in Lamang

Although slavery is outlawed worldwide, Lamang continues to practice methods that strongly resemble it.

Reports indicate that foreign workers arrive in Lamang seeking well-paid jobs, only to have their passports confiscated and be forced into labor, often working merely for food and water. Women are primarily coerced into prostitution, while men are exploited in construction and digital fraud schemes.


Interview — Patricia Bell, Ph.D.

Q: Dr. Bell, as an expert on Southeast Asia, what can you tell us about the current state of crime in Lamang?

A: It’s extremely difficult to get a clear picture inside a closed-off country like Lamang. As with many authoritarian regimes, they make investigations nearly impossible and suppress information that might expose their wrongdoing. From what little we do know, they’ve likely struck some kind of arrangement with criminal networks operating out of the Golden Triangle. And that’s not even accounting for the other criminal activities happening within the country.

Q: So you believe the Lamangese government is cooperating with Golden Triangle crime syndicates?

A: Not necessarily in a direct sense, but certainly in an indirect one. I believe they rely on local criminal groups to carry out illicit operations, while the government maintains an appearance of defending traditional values and national order.

Q: Is there any realistic way to hold them accountable?

A: Unless you’re prepared to support military intervention, which I’m not suggesting, then unfortunately, no. For now, all we can do is wait. If the regime eventually collapses, there may be a window to uncover the truth and bring justice for the crimes committed.

Q: Thank you for sharing your insights, Dr. Bell.

A: You’re welcome.

Covid Hits Lamang Hard

June 6, 2020

The Democratic Republic of Lamang is struggling to contain the rapid spread of COVID-19. Reports suggest thousands have died and many more are infected.

“I can guarantee you we have no issue with this so-called disease. If you in the West are having a problem, that’s on you; don’t project it onto us. We’re completely fine,” said Vice President Jan Park in response to our questions.

The true scale of the crisis in Lamang is difficult to determine, but given the regime’s track record in handling emergencies, it’s safe to assume they are facing serious problems.


Poland Invades Czech Republic!

For several days, a small Czech chapel near the Czech–Polish border was inadvertently occupied by the Polish army.

Once higher authorities became aware of the situation, they promptly issued an apology and withdrew from Czech territory.


Interview — Patricia Bell, Ph.D.

Q: Dr. Bell, what can you tell us about the current situation in Lamang?

A: It seems the Lamangese government has badly underestimated the threat of COVID-19. In that sense, it’s quite similar to North Korea. They’ll almost certainly blame the outside world for their own mistakes and lack of preparation. Sadly, it’s the ordinary people who will bear the consequences.

Q: Do you think they’ll ask for help?

A: No, they won’t. Authoritarian regimes like Lamang’s must project strength at all times. And even if, by some miracle, they accept assistance, they’ll present it as if it were their own act of generosity.

Q: Do you expect many casualties in Lamang?

A: Unfortunately, yes. Lamang has never had a large population, and this outbreak could further reduce the already small number of inhabitants.

Q: Thank you for sharing your insights, Dr. Bell.

A: You’re very welcome.

KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_crooksDiary_Page_1:
November 25, 2025

Dear Diary,

Today was so strange. Mama said we were going on a big trip, so we came to the airfield. It was really loud, and there were lots of people everywhere, way more than the last time we flew. But no one looked happy.

I saw a man running, holding so many bags that he dropped one, and stuff went everywhere. He looked so scared. A lady tried to help him pick it up, but he yelled at her and kept running. Why was he so mad? Mama pulled me closer and told me not to stare, but I couldn’t stop looking.

There was a baby crying so loud. It made my ears hurt. The mama was holding the baby so tight, and she kept looking around like she was waiting for something bad to happen. I asked Mama if the baby was okay, but she just said Shh.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_crooksDiary_Page_2:
Some people were yelling at a man in a uniform. He didn’t yell back, just kept waving his hands and pointing. His face was so red. One man tried to push past him, but two other men came and made him stop.

Mama was squeezing my hand really hard, so I asked if she was mad. She shook her head but didn’t say anything.

I wanted to ask Mama why everyone looked so scared, but she told me to be quiet. Then she picked me up and hugged me tight. Her heart was beating really fast, and her arms were shaking a little.

I don’t think I like it here anymore.

TOP SECRET

Preliminary Status Report

Data currently received from the research station indicate that Project Distant Voice demonstrates a high probability of eventual success. While precise temporal parameters cannot yet be established, current models suggest that realization may occur on a long-term scale, potentially several decades from the present date.

Given the sensitivity of the findings, all further communication with Moscow must be conducted with the utmost discretion and strictly through approved secure channels. The scope and potential implications of this project extend beyond immediate strategic value and may have long-term consequences for humanity as a whole.

Operational security remains a critical priority. Any disclosure of projected outcomes or theoretical conclusions to hostile entities must be prevented, as premature exposure could compromise both the research and broader strategic stability.

Ongoing technical analysis continues in the areas of signal propagation, systemic resonance, and long-range informational transmission. Although results remain preliminary, no deviations from expected theoretical behavior have been observed to date.

End of Report

00:31
Keon and Teyvada returned from Ground Zero less than two hours ago. Both were walking, but complained of nausea, vertigo, and intense heat under the skin.

03:24 
Symptoms escalated rapidly. Skin discoloration spreading. Tissue feels soft, fluid, like it won’t hold together. I have never seen anything like this before. Radiation sickness does not progress like this. I’m trying to help with what little I have. No effect.

05:10
Teyvada is gone. Cellular breakdown visible to the naked eye. Skin separating. Muscle structure failing. I don’t think this is radiation as we understand it. It’s too fast. Like the body is being erased.

5:48
Keon just died. We have to leave. Whatever’s left of us. We have to leave now. Let the Vietnamese take over. They were letting us be because they needed a doctor, but I don’t care anymore. If we stay here with the bodies, we’re as good as dead anyway.

KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_PAP_VietnameseReport_Page_1:
Nam, the radios are all fucked here. We can’t hear shit apart from some garbled noise, so I’m sending Phúc to hand you this report.

Nobody was in the village when we arrived. Guess the crowd Lý saw walking south last night really did come from here. Shit’s weird, though. It’s like they all suddenly stopped whatever they were doing and just left. No idea why. Some of the guys are complaining their ears started ringing the moment we got here. Maybe there’s something around that drove them off? With the radios acting up too, I’ve got a bad feeling about this place. Some of the things we’re picking up are weird as fuck. I’m not joking.

Send Phúc back ASAP with your orders, but if it were up to me, I’d just leave. There’s nothing here worth taking anyway, and the strategic value is minimal at best.

Minh

KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_VillagersDiary_Page_1:
It’s been days now. The ringing won’t stop. I can’t think. I can’t sleep. Some hear it too, but not as loud. I don’t think anymore. I have to write my thoughts down. They won’t stay in my head. I can’t think. What do I do?

Voices. Voices in the tone. Louder and louder. Everybody hears it now. Some pretend they don’t, but I know. It will only get worse. I can’t hear anything else. Just the tone. Voices. Tone. Voices. Am I going to die?

Voices calling. Have to go. All have to go. We all go. It wants us. It needs us. Don’t be afraid. There is no need to panic. We all have to go.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_PAP_PsychoticNote_Page_1: I only wish to see her again. If I go, I’ll see her again. I know this. Can I see her again? I will see her again. I can’t wait to see her again. She’s waiting for me there. All alone. I will go and see her again. That is my wish. That is my only wish. I wish to see her again, and if I go, I will see her again. If only I could see her again. I wish I will wish to see her again. What if I don’t see her again? What if I don’t wish to see her again? I have to wish to see her again. I have to wish to see her again. I wish to see her again. I have to wish to see her again. I wish to see her again. I wish to see her again. I wish to see her again. I wish to see her again. I wish to see her again.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_FL_LAFCodebook_Page_1:
The following pages provide a comprehensive description of the coded communication methods for the current operation. These materials are classified and intended solely for authorized personnel. All selected soldiers are required to study the contents carefully and understand their proper use, as this knowledge is critical to our success.

All codebooks and related materials are to be kept under constant, heavy guard. At no point may they fall into enemy hands. Any compromise of these codes would be devastating to our operational efforts, granting hostile forces insight into our movements, intentions, and command structure. The security of these materials is therefore of the highest priority and must be treated as such at all times.

The command requires absolute discipline when handling this information. Failure to safeguard these assets will result in capital punishment.

At present, the most important code to memorize is: President Narith Sayavong = Celestial Charioteer.

To learn more about coded communication, please refer to the attachments: 2a, 2b, 2c, 3a, 3b, and 3c.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_UnknownSoldiersNotes_Page_1:
Taking this camp from those lunatics was a fucking mistake. We all said it, but the boss didn’t care. Of course he didn’t. Something broke in him after that failed attack on Fort Narith. It’s like he’s obsessed now. I honestly think he wouldn’t hesitate to sacrifice every last one of us if it meant getting closer to that fuckhead Banshee.

We took this place to “bolster our control of the area,” and all it did was get more people killed and piss off the local “rebels” something fierce. And for what? A handful of decrepit buildings rotting in the jungle. Fucking great.
_____________________________________________

Another request for reinforcements denied. We are so fucked. I knew this entire job sounded too good to be true. Fuck me.
_____________________________________________

Some shit is spreading in the main camp. People are getting sick. Honestly, I’m starting to feel glad I’m stuck out here instead of over there. Fucking silver lining, I guess. I just hope the doc knows what he’s doing. Poor bastard has his work cut out for him, that’s for sure.
_____________________________________________
I know those fuckers are moving around us. They don’t even bother hiding it anymore. They’ll attack soon. I’m sure of it. What the fuck are we supposed to do?
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_UnknownSoldiersNotes_Page_2:
It fucking happened.

Those bastards tried to take back their camp this morning. It was a bloodbath. They came from all sides, lobbing grenades like it was nothing. I swear I saw some of them charge straight at our positions like maniacs and blow themselves up.

Fucking insane.

I still don’t know how we managed to drive them back, but we did. More dead. More wounded. We’re bleeding out and can’t do a damn thing about it, while they’ll just regroup and hit us again. After all, this is their home.

I can’t stay here anymore. I have to do something.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_PAP_BlackBoxNote_Page_1: As per the agreement, our friends from the jungle took possession of the black box. They provided us with info about that motherfucker Banshee. That fucking snake will soon taste his own blood. Our agreement with them has ended, so shoot them on sight if you encounter them again. You can stop guarding the shack.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_PAP_BlackBoxCode_Page_1:
Rithy, you fucking dick! I don't care if you're injured. If I catch you sleeping on guard duty again, I'll report you to Kaptei. We need to deliver the black box safely, and you know it. And don't you dare forget the code!

{Password}
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_PirateBossDiary_Page_1:
FUCKING LAMANG ARMY! Getting in my way! Fuck them all!

Tiger Bay was supposed to be easy. Quick hit, grab what we need, get out. Now they’ve dug in and turned it into a fortress. Cost me time. Cost me men.

Sabtu bin Panjang doesn’t get cockblocked by anyone. The cunts back in Malaysia know it, and now these one will know it too! They're dead. All of them.

We found an empty village nearby. We’ll sit on it for now and watch the bay. Someone else will take a swing at Tiger Bay eventually. When the cunts start bleeding, that’s when we move. No point burning more people until then.

The idiots nearly pissed themselves when they came into this place. Whispering about ghosts and curses like children. Superstitious motherfuckers.

Funny thing is, once we figured out what the villagers were really doing here, everyone's mood shot up.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_PAP_LLAInternalComms_Page_1:
Inventory confirms receipt of personnel and materiel transferred from The Hole as scheduled. Maintain readiness and limit unnecessary engagements.

Local resistance in the area remains weakened. Any hostile elements encountered are to be neutralized quickly and quietly. Do not pursue beyond immediate objectives.

Continue monitoring activity around the sawmill. Further instructions will follow once conditions are deemed favorable.

Ensure all written records remain secured. Loose information compromises the cause.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_PAP_LLAOperationalNote_Page_1:
Sahai,

The people we have liberated must understand that freedom demands sacrifice. Not all are ready to fight with weapons in their hands, but every Lamangese can still serve the cause.

Those who cannot be trained or armed in time are to be prepared for other forms of contribution. Their willingness to give themselves for the future of this island will inspire fear in our enemies and courage in our comrades.

Do not speak of these measures outside approved circles. The weak would misunderstand what is necessary, and the occupiers would seek to twist it into propaganda. History will judge us differently.

Remind the people that their lives already belong to Lamang. To hesitate now is to betray those who have already given everything.

Free Lamang!
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_PAP_LLAStrategicDirective_Page_:
Sahai,

The time for preparation is nearing its end.

With The Hole secured as our primary stronghold, forward positions such as this camp exist to support the next phase of liberation. The enemy believes the jungle and distance will shield them. They are mistaken.

Once resistance around the sawmill is eliminated, the site will be repurposed as a staging ground. Its location and infrastructure make it ideal for movement of personnel and materiel toward Fort Narith. Pressure from the south will coincide with an assault launched from The Hole itself. The tyrant's dogs will be forced to divide their attention, and in that moment, they will break.

All units are to maintain readiness and await final authorization. No action is to be taken prematurely. Discipline and unity will decide the outcome more than numbers ever could.

The people have already given much. Soon, they will give what is required to finish this.

Free Lamang!
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_PAP_DrugOPMessage_Page_1:
The boys are working around the clock, pulling the goods out of the hidden stashes. Sad sada keeps insisting the sooner we’re gone, the better. What happened here was a sign, it’s time to leave Lamang.

This place was meant only as a temporary storage spot, but now it’s packed to the ceiling with product. I keep worrying the state might raid us, but they’ve probably got bigger problems to deal with right now.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_ShamansDiary_Page_1:
I became Sad sada as part of the plan, but somewhere along the way, something changed. Either I’ve grown soft with time, or some higher power is whispering in my ear, telling me to do the right thing. Maybe it’s Phi Pa Mum himself.

I ordered the men to leave part of the product behind so we could evacuate everyone. Unfortunately, the more zealous members of our “congregation” rebelled against my decision. They left, who knows where.

I think I might be losing my mind, but it is the truth: I truly believe what happened here was a sign from Phi Pa Mum, a warning to abandon this island before he takes revenge on the corrupt Lamang state.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_PAP_LLARecruitmentDirective_Pag:
Sahai,

All villages brought under our protection must be reorganized to serve the struggle. Civilians willing to join the ranks are to be processed immediately and directed toward training cadres. Their enthusiasm must be cultivated, their loyalty reinforced, and their families assured that their sacrifice is honorable and necessary.

Those who hesitate are not enemies. They are simply unprepared for the weight of freedom. Political officers are instructed to engage them personally, to explain the crimes of the regime, and to remind them that neutrality is no longer possible in a land already soaked in blood.

However, refusal to stand with Lamang cannot be tolerated.

Individuals who actively resist recruitment or attempt to undermine morale are to be removed swiftly and publicly. Their fate is to be framed as the tragic consequence of collaboration with the occupiers and betrayal of the people. Such measures are regrettable, but essential to maintain momentum and unity.

Villages are to be marked clearly following successful reorganization. Revolutionary slogans and banners are to be displayed prominently so that all may know who now protects them.

Stay strong, stay united.

Free Lamang!
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_BanSukCodeBook_Page_1:
Leilanie,

That quack really thinks everyone in here is eating his shit right up. I've been watching him closely for some time now, and he's nothing but a phony. I even managed to get the code to his "meditation chamber." {Password}.

Soon, I will reveal everything, but if anything happens to me or I have to leave, I hope you find this notebook and do it in my stead.

Remember what we talked about: no more false prophets, no more miracle remedies, no more snake-oil salesmen. Not after what happened to Mom.

I love you, sis. I'm sure you'll do the right thing, and we'll see each other again.

Priority remains on foreign tourists. Preference for individuals without local ties or extended travel parties.

Holding sites are to be used strictly for short-term containment and screening. Assets are not to be kept in poor conditions. Visible injuries reduce value and draw unwanted scrutiny.

All assets must be cleaned, restrained, and sedated prior to transfer.

Identification documents are to be copied and archived before movement.

Any losses during capture or transport are to be logged immediately.

Delivery thresholds remain unchanged.

MISSING:

Two prop shafts
Fuel pump assemblies (x3)

We’re bleeding fuel faster than we can siphon it. Half the cans we brought were contaminated. Saltwater in one of the tanks too. Idiots. If we don’t finish at least two boats this week, we’re done. Gunfire every day. Fucking helicopters buzzing overhead. What the fuck is happening? I don't want to die here. I know what they were doing to those people wasn't right, but I didn't touch them. I didn't do anything! I don't deserve this. Why didn't they let us leave? Fuck.

KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_NB_FarmersDiary_Page_1:
They left us here. I don’t know why. I don’t know what we’ve done. Maybe they just forgot. The fact is, we’re on our own now. We still have some supplies, but they won’t last long. Thank God we know how to tend the land.
.........
Bugs. Bugs everyhwhere. Eating all the crop. I knew I should have ordered more pesticide, but I thought there still was time. We can't leave the farm. Here we are secluded, out of everyone's way. Safe. But out there? Who knows what's happening anywhere else.
.........
That madman Maik did it. He drove in the middle of the night all the way to the Khamhao rice depot to pick up pesticide. And not some weak stuff either. This is meant for the government, so it has to be the best shit ever. He says it was easy. He says he’ll make a few more trips so we have enough for the future. Who knows how long all this will last, or if it will ever end. We have to be prepared.
.........
The stuff is doing wonders! We are saved! I had my doubts, but now I am sure. We will make it through this.
KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_FL_LAFUNLRADeal_Page_1:
Directive Regarding Non-Engagement with UNLRA Forces

Date: November 28, 2025

Attention!

Effective immediately, all LAF military personnel are hereby ordered to refrain from any form of engagement—verbal, physical, or armed—with UNLRA forces operating within or near our designated area of operations.

This directive is issued in accordance with the Bilateral Security Coordination Agreement (BSCA), which outlines mutual cooperation, neutrality, and coordinated deconfliction procedures between the Democratic Republic of Lamang and UNLRA.

All UNLRA personnel have been informed and instructed to cooperate with the LAF and to prohibit the evacuation of specific dangerous individuals and people from affected areas (Fort Narith and its vicinity).

This order remains in effect until formally rescinded or amended.

Rejoice, for you have been chosen to be the tip of the spear that will pierce the hearts of tyrants and occupiers smothering our beautiful land. Know that your sacrifice will not be in vain, but a seed from which a red rose of freedom shall bloom. Though you may not be here to see the bright future you helped build, your memory and spirit shall live on. You are a true hero.

KeysToEntries.IS_Intel_PAP_GeneratorStartupInstruction:
Hey, new guy. Sorry I couldn't welcome you personally, but my services were required elsewhere. I'm sure you'll do just fine, but just in case, here's how you get the power on:

1. Start the generator first.
Let it run until the RPM settles and the output stabilizes. You want steady frequency and voltage before you throw any load.

2. THEN switch breakers in this order: 2, 3, 1, 4.
This staggers the inrush and keeps the weakest circuits from pulling the whole system down.

3. If a breaker pops, DO NOT hammer it back on.
Kill the load, wait a few seconds, then repeat the sequence from the top.

Good luck!

Oasis of life has been muddied by the greater will as new opportunities arise in the hearth.
